cb6eba52a342.name: How to Add a Notification Service Extension in an iOS Project and Implement Image Push Notification Handling
cb6eba52a342.description: A guide on how to add a Notification Service Extension in iOS 10 and above projects, and write code to download and display push notification images as well as report push notification display statistics.

step.name: Step 1: Add Notification Service Extension Target
step.text: Open Xcode, click the File menu, select New, then choose Target. Under the iOS category, select Notification Service Extension and click Next. Enter the product name, click Finish, and finally click Activate to enable this Target.

step.name: Step 2: Implement NotificationService.swift to Handle Push Content
step.text: Open the newly added NotificationService.swift file and implement the didReceive method of UNNotificationServiceExtension. First, copy the push content and retrieve the custom fields push_id and image from userInfo.

step.name: Step 3: Send Push Display Statistics API Request
step.text: In the didReceive method, if push_id is obtained, create a URLRequest pointing to the display statistics API, set the HTTP method to POST, and include push_id as a parameter. Use URLSession's dataTask to asynchronously send the request to report that the push notification has been displayed.

step.name: Step 4: Download Push Notification Image and Attach to Notification
step.text: If the image field contains an image URL, use URLSession's dataTask to download the image data, save the image to a temporary directory, create a UNNotificationAttachment, and add the attachment to the notification content's attachments. If the body is empty, set a default text 'View Now'.

step.name: Step 5: Call contentHandler to Complete Push Content Processing
step.text: When the image download completes or an error occurs, call contentHandler(bestAttemptContent) to return the modified push content so the system can display the updated notification. If the 30-second limit is approaching, call contentHandler in the serviceExtensionTimeWillExpire method to ensure the notification displays properly.

step.name: Step 6: Verify Successful Display of Image Push Notification and Report Statistics
step.text: After sending the push notification, confirm the device receives the notification and displays the image attachment, and check whether the backend received the API request reporting the push display statistics. If the push notification title includes the text [modified], it indicates the Notification Service Extension has executed successfully.

9a9aa892f9a9.name: How to Automatically Detect Faces and Crop Profile Pictures in iOS Apps Using the Vision Framework
9a9aa892f9a9.description: This tutorial demonstrates how to use the iOS Vision framework to detect face locations in photos and crop suitable profile pictures centered on the face.

step.name: Step 1: Adjust UIImageView Size and Image Aspect Ratio
step.text: First, obtain the screen width as the width reference, use the KingFisher library to scale the original image according to the width, set the UIImageView's contentMode to redraw, and adjust the UIImageView's constraints so its height matches the scaled image height, preventing image distortion or blank spaces.

step.name: Step 2: Detect Face Locations and Mark Them Using the Vision Framework
step.text: Convert the UIImage to CIImage, create a VNDetectFaceRectanglesRequest request, and retrieve face observation results in the completion handler. Convert the face boundingBox coordinates from the original image coordinate system to the UIImageView coordinate system, then add a semi-transparent green UIView on the main thread to indicate the face locations.

step.name: Step 3: Crop the Image into a Profile Picture Based on the Face Center
step.text: Use Vision to detect a single face, obtain the face boundingBox and convert it to a fixed size coordinate system (e.g., 300x300). Calculate the offset of the face center relative to the cropping area, then use KingFisher's resize and crop functions to crop the image centered on the face into the profile picture size, and update the UIImageView's image.

step.name: Step 4: Verify Successful Cropping of the Profile Picture
step.text: Check the cropped UIImageView in the app to confirm the image is cropped according to the face center with correct size, ensuring the face is fully visible within the profile picture area without distortion or cropping errors.

793bf2cdda0f.name: How to Train a Text Classification Model Yourself Using CoreML 2.0
793bf2cdda0f.description: This tutorial demonstrates how to prepare training data, use third-party tools to train a CoreML text classification model, and integrate the model into an iOS project for prediction.

step.name: Step 1: Export Training Data as JSON Format
step.text: Use Phpmyadmin to export the MySQL table posts by executing the SQL command SELECT `title` AS `text`,`type` AS `label` FROM `posts` WHERE `status` = '1', and change the export format to JSON.

step.name: Step 2: Clean the JSON File Content
step.text: Open the exported JSON file and delete all information except the data structure. Keep only the text and label data inside the data array to ensure the format meets the training tool requirements.

step.name: Step 3: Train the Model Using TextClassiferPlayground
step.text: Download and open TextClassifer.playground, click the red run button to start the Playground, drag the organized JSON training data into the GUI tool, and observe the Console below for training progress until the phrase "Test Accuracy" appears, indicating training completion.

step.name: Step 4: Save the Trained CoreML Model File
step.text: Fill in the basic model information in the training tool, then click the save button to store the trained .mlmodel file locally.

step.name: Step 5: Add the Model File to the iOS Project
step.text: Open Xcode and drag or add the saved .mlmodel file into the iOS project to complete model integration.

step.name: Step 6: Write Code to Use the Model for Text Classification Prediction
step.text: In Swift code, import CoreML, and use if #available(iOS 12.0, *), let prediction = try? textClassifier().prediction(text: "Text content to predict") to get the prediction result, then access prediction.label to obtain the classification label.

step.name: Step 7: Verify Whether the Model Successfully Performs Text Classification Prediction
step.text: Run the iOS app, input test text content, and observe whether the Console output correctly displays the predicted classification label. Successful display indicates the model training and integration were successful.

1ca246e27273.name: How to Add 3D Touch Functionality in an iOS APP
1ca246e27273.description: Teach you how to implement 3D Touch Preview ViewController preview functionality and APP shortcut launch features in an iOS APP to enhance user experience.

step.name: Step 1: Create UIViewControllerPreviewable Protocol
step.text: Create a UIViewControllerPreviewable protocol in your project, defining a Boolean property is3DTouchPreview to determine whether it is currently in 3D Touch preview mode. The code is as follows: protocol UIViewControllerPreviewable { var is3DTouchPreview:Bool {get set} }

step.name: Step 2: Implement Preview Determination in Target ViewController
step.text: Make the target ViewController conform to the UIViewControllerPreviewable protocol, and in viewDidLoad, determine whether it is preview mode based on the is3DTouchPreview property, adjusting the display accordingly, such as hiding the toolbar or showing full screen.

step.name: Step 3: Register 3D Touch Support in List View
step.text: Override the traitCollectionDidChange method in the UIViewController of the list view, check if traitCollection.forceTouchCapability is available, and if so, call registerForPreviewing(with: self, sourceView: self.TableView) or CollectionView to register 3D Touch preview.

step.name: Step 4: Implement UIViewControllerPreviewingDelegate Methods
step.text: Extend UIViewControllerPreviewingDelegate in the UIViewController of the list view, implement the previewingContext(_:viewControllerForLocation:) method to get the indexPath and cell at the touch location, create the target ViewController, set the preview area and is3DTouchPreview property, and in the previewingContext(_:commit:) method, set is3DTouchPreview to false and push the target ViewController.

step.name: Step 5: Add APP Shortcut Settings in info.plist
step.text: Open info.plist, add an array UIApplicationShortcutItems, add a Dictionary item, set UIApplicationShortcutItemType and UIApplicationShortcutItemTitle as required, and optionally set UIApplicationShortcutItemSubtitle, UIApplicationShortcutItemIconType, or UIApplicationShortcutItemIconFile to complete the APP shortcut menu settings.

step.name: Step 6: Implement Shortcut Handling Function in AppDelegate
step.text: Add the application(_:performActionFor:completionHandler:) method in AppDelegate, determine the shortcut type the user clicked based on shortcutItem.type, execute the corresponding logic, and finally call completionHandler(true) to indicate completion.

step.name: Step 7: Verify Success
step.text: Run the APP on a device that supports 3D Touch, long press a 3D Touch supported cell on the list page, confirm whether the preview window pops up and the background blurs, and after releasing, it can enter the target page normally; at the same time, long press the APP icon, confirm whether the shortcut menu appears and clicking it executes the corresponding function correctly.

a4bc3bce7513.name: How to Save and Retrieve UUID Using Key-Chain on iOS
a4bc3bce7513.description: This article explains how to use Swift on iOS to save and read the device's unique identifier UUID through Key-Chain, preventing UUID changes caused by deleting and reinstalling the app.

step.name: Step 1: Read UUID from Key-Chain
step.text: Use Swift to create a query dictionary, setting kSecClass to kSecClassGenericPassword, kSecAttrAccount to "DEVICE_UUID", kSecReturnData to kCFBooleanTrue, and kSecMatchLimit to kSecMatchLimitOne. Call the SecItemCopyMatching function to attempt to read data from the Key-Chain. If successful, convert the data to a string type UUID.

step.name: Step 2: Obtain identifierForVendor if UUID is not in Key-Chain
step.text: If SecItemCopyMatching returns an error or no data, use UIDevice.current.identifierForVendor?.uuidString to get the system-provided UUID. If it is nil, generate a new UUID using UUID().uuidString.

step.name: Step 3: Write the obtained UUID into Key-Chain
step.text: Convert the obtained UUID string to UTF-8 encoded Data, create a query dictionary setting kSecClass to kSecClassGenericPassword, kSecAttrAccount to "DEVICE_UUID", and kSecValueData to that Data. First call SecItemDelete to remove old data, then call SecItemAdd to add the UUID to the Key-Chain.

step.name: Step 4: Verify successful retrieval and saving of UUID
step.text: After running the above code, call SecItemCopyMatching again to read "DEVICE_UUID" from the Key-Chain. Confirm that the UUID string can be successfully retrieved, indicating the UUID has been saved successfully and can be reused, preventing UUID changes caused by deleting and reinstalling the app.

ade9e745a4bf.name: How to Obtain Provisional Push Notification Permission on iOS 12 Using Swift
ade9e745a4bf.description: This guide explains how to request provisional push notification permission for UserNotifications using Swift on iOS 12, and describes how to set up and verify silent notifications.

step.name: Step 1: Add Code to Request Provisional Notification Permission in AppDelegate didFinishLaunchingWithOptions
step.text: Add the following code in the didFinishLaunchingWithOptions method of AppDelegate: use UNUserNotificationCenter.current() to get the notification center and call requestAuthorization with options including .badge, .alert, .sound, and .provisional. This requests provisional notification permission without showing a prompt.

step.name: Step 2: Run the App and Confirm No Notification Authorization Prompt Appears
step.text: After running the app, observe whether a notification authorization prompt appears. When requesting provisional permission with the above code, the system does not show a prompt, indicating that silent notification permission has been successfully obtained.

step.name: Step 3: Check Notification Permission Status in Code to Confirm Provisional Permission
step.text: Use UNUserNotificationCenter.current().getNotificationSettings to get the notification settings and check if settings.authorizationStatus is .provisional, which means provisional notification permission is currently granted.

step.name: Step 4: Verify Silent Notification Settings for the App in iOS Settings
step.text: Open iOS Settings, tap Notifications, find the target app, and confirm that all notification permissions are off except for the 'Notification Center' option. This means the app’s notifications are silent, with no banners, sounds, or lock screen display.

step.name: Step 5: Verify Successful Acquisition of Provisional Notification Permission
step.text: While the app is running, use code to check that the notification permission status is .provisional, and verify in iOS Settings Notification Center that the app has silent notifications. This confirms successful acquisition of provisional push notification permission.

fd7f92d52baa.name: How to Handle Push Notification Permission Status on iOS 9 to iOS 12
fd7f92d52baa.description: This tutorial explains how to detect push notification permission status, request permission, and handle responses accordingly on iOS 9 to iOS 12 devices. It includes using Observable to monitor permission changes and directing users to the settings page to enable notifications.

step.name: Step 1: Declare a Global Notification Permission Status Object
step.text: Declare a global notificationStatus object in the project with the type Observable<NotificationStatusType?>, and define the NotificationStatusType enum including authorized, denied, and notDetermined states to store the push notification permission status.

step.name: Step 2: Implement a Method to Check Notification Permission Status
step.text: Write the checkNotificationPermissionStatus() method. For iOS 10 and above, use UNUserNotificationCenter.current().getNotificationSettings to get the permission status. For iOS 9 and below, determine the status using UIApplication.shared.currentUserNotificationSettings?.types, combined with a custom UserDefaults key "iOS9NotificationIsDetermined" to distinguish whether the user has been asked before.

step.name: Step 3: Call the Check Method in AppDelegate
step.text: Call checkNotificationPermissionStatus() in AppDelegate's didFinishLaunchingWithOptions and applicationDidBecomeActive methods to ensure the push notification permission status is updated promptly when the app launches or returns from the background.

step.name: Step 4: Implement a Method to Request Notification Permission
step.text: Write the requestNotificationPermission() method. For iOS 10 and above, use UNUserNotificationCenter.current().requestAuthorization to request permission. For iOS 9 and below, call application.registerUserNotificationSettings, and update UserDefaults and recheck permission status in AppDelegate's didRegisterUserNotificationSettings method.

step.name: Step 5: Prompt the User and Redirect to Settings Based on Permission Status
step.text: On the necessary pages, check notificationStatus.value. If it is denied, present a UIAlertController prompting the user to go to the App settings page to enable notification permission, and use UIApplication.shared.openURL(URL(string:UIApplicationOpenSettingsURLString + bundleID)) to perform the redirect, avoiding the use of Private API.

step.name: Step 6: Monitor Notification Permission Status Changes for Dynamic Handling
step.text: In the ViewController where real-time monitoring of permission status is needed, add a listener to notificationStatus.afterChange in viewDidLoad. Perform corresponding actions based on the new permission status, such as showing a thank-you message or reminding the user.

step.name: Step 7: Verify Successful Update of Notification Permission Status
step.text: Run the app and test under different permission statuses, including the initial permission request, denial followed by prompt and successful redirect to the settings page. After returning to the app, the permission status should update immediately, indicating the push notification permission status handling process is successful.

f644db1bb8bf.name: How to Add an App Notification Settings Shortcut on iOS 12 and Above
f644db1bb8bf.description: This guide shows you how to enable users to see a shortcut to your app's notification settings page within the system Settings on iOS 12 and above, and provide an "In-App Settings" option when receiving notifications.

step.name: Step 1: Add providesAppNotificationSettings Permission When Requesting Push Notification Authorization
step.text: In the AppDelegate's didFinishLaunchingWithOptions method, use UNUserNotificationCenter.current() to get the notification center and call requestAuthorization. Include .providesAppNotificationSettings in the authorization options so the system knows your app provides a notification settings page.

step.name: Step 2: Set AppDelegate as UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate and Implement openSettingsFor Method
step.text: Conform to the UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate protocol in AppDelegate, and set UNUserNotificationCenter.current().delegate = self in didFinishLaunchingWithOptions. Implement the userNotificationCenter(_:openSettingsFor:) method to navigate to the in-app notification settings page.

step.name: Step 3: Verify the App Notification Settings Shortcut is Displayed Successfully
step.text: On your iOS device, go to Settings -> Your App -> Notifications and confirm that the "In-App Settings" shortcut option appears. When receiving a notification, use 3D Touch to long-press the notification and verify that the "In-App Settings" option is available.

e85d77b05061.name: How to Build an Apple Watch App
e85d77b05061.description: This tutorial demonstrates how to add a watchOS Target from an iOS project, design the Apple Watch App's UI layout, program control, data transmission, and push notification handling.

step.name: Step 1: Create a watchOS Target
step.text: In Xcode, select File -> New -> Target, choose WatchKit App under the watchOS category, and add a watchOS Target. This Target includes both the WatchKit App (resources and UI) and the WatchKit Extension (program logic), both of which are essential.

step.name: Step 2: Design UI layout and view controllers
step.text: In the WatchKit App's Interface.storyboard, locate the Interface Controller Scene, and use Group components to set up the layout, including parameters such as Layout, Insets, Spacing, Radius, Alignment, and Size. For example, for the Line App main page, add a WKInterfaceTable and design the Row layout style and Identifier.

step.name: Step 3: Implement program control and data binding
step.text: Create a Class corresponding to the Row in the WatchKit Extension and connect components using IBOutlet. In InterfaceController.swift, create an Outlet linked to the Table and implement the loadData() method to set the number of rows and Row content. Implement table(_:didSelectRowAt:) to handle Row selection events.

step.name: Step 4: Set up data synchronization with iPhone
step.text: Use the WatchConnectivity framework to implement communication between the phone and watch. On the phone side, start WatchSessionManager in AppDelegate; on the watch side, start it in ExtensionDelegate. Implement WCSessionDelegate methods to handle data sending and receiving, using sendMessage, transferUserInfo, and other methods to transmit data.

step.name: Step 5: Implement watchOS push notification functionality
step.text: In the WatchKit Extension's ExtensionDelegate.swift, implement UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate, set the delegate, and handle push notification display and tap events. Use PushNotificationPayload.apns to test push notifications in the simulator. Customize the NotificationController class to design dynamic push notification interfaces and set reply or view buttons based on the notification type.

step.name: Step 6: Verify the Apple Watch App is working successfully
step.text: Deploy the project to the Apple Watch simulator or a physical device, launch the App, confirm the UI layout displays correctly, the Table loads data properly, and responds to selection events. Test data synchronization between the phone and watch, and use simulated push notification files to test push notification display and interactive buttons.

ac557047d206.name: How to Implement Your Own Call Identification Feature in an iOS Project
ac557047d206.description: Implement incoming call identification and phone number labeling in an iOS app using Call Directory Extension, including adding the Extension, maintaining a local identification database, notifying refreshes, and writing Extension code.

step.name: Step 1: Add Call Directory Extension in Xcode
step.text: Open Xcode, select File -> New -> Target, choose Call Directory Extension, enter the Extension name, and optionally add a Scheme for easier debugging. After completion, a folder and code for the Call Directory Extension will appear in the project directory.

step.name: Step 2: Maintain the Local Call Identification Database
step.text: In the main iOS project, use FileManager to access the Group directory shared across Extensions, create or read the phoneIdentity.json file, which stores a dictionary of phone numbers and names. Use JSONSerialization to convert the dictionary into JSON data and write it to the file, ensuring the Extension can also read this file.

step.name: Step 3: Notify Call Directory Extension to Reload Data
step.text: Use the CXCallDirectoryManager.sharedInstance.reloadExtension(withIdentifier:) method, passing in the Bundle Identifier of the Call Directory Extension. After calling, the system will execute the Extension's beginRequest method. This method refreshes the identification database and allows checking success or errors in the completionHandler.

step.name: Step 4: Implement the beginRequest Method in Call Directory Extension
step.text: Open CallDirectoryHandler.swift and implement the addAllIdentificationPhoneNumbers method. Read phoneIdentity.json from the Group directory shared across Extensions, parse phone numbers and labels, and sequentially call context.addIdentificationEntry(withNextSequentialPhoneNumber:label:) to add identification data. Ensure data is sorted and old data is removed first to avoid reload failures.

step.name: Step 5: Verify Successful Activation of Call Identification Feature
step.text: In iOS Settings, go to Settings -> Phone -> Call Blocking & Identification and confirm your app is enabled. You can also check if the Extension is enabled programmatically using CXCallDirectoryManager.sharedInstance.getEnabledStatusForExtension(withIdentifier:). When making or receiving calls, if your custom call identification label appears, the feature has been successfully implemented.

c5e7e580c341.name: How to Implement One-Time Offers or Trials Using DeviceCheck on iOS
c5e7e580c341.description: This tutorial explains how to obtain a temporary DeviceCheck token on the iOS app side, configure the Apple developer account's Team ID, Key ID, and private key on the backend, assemble a JWT, and query or write the device's 2-bit status on Apple's server to implement one-time offers or trial features.

step.name: Step 1: Obtain the DeviceCheck Temporary Token on the iOS App
step.text: Use Swift in the iOS app to generate a temporary device token via DCDevice.current.generateToken, then encode this deviceToken in base64 and prepare to send it to the backend API.

step.name: Step 2: Log in to Apple Developer Portal and Record Team ID
step.text: Log in to the Apple Developer Portal https://developer.apple.com/account/#/membership/, find and note your Team ID on the membership page, which will be used later when assembling the JWT.

step.name: Step 3: Create a DeviceCheck-Specific Key and Download the Private Key
step.text: In the Apple Developer Portal, go to Certificates, IDs & Profiles, select Keys, click the "+" button at the top right to add a new key, check the DeviceCheck permission, then note the Key ID and download the privateKey.p8 file.

step.name: Step 4: Assemble the JWT Authorization String
step.text: According to Apple specifications, use the ES256 algorithm to assemble the JWT HEADER (including alg and kid) and PAYLOAD (including iss, iat, exp timestamps), then sign with the privateKey.p8 to generate the JWT string.

step.name: Step 5: Query or Update Device Status on Apple DeviceCheck Server
step.text: Use the assembled JWT string as the Authorization Bearer to send a POST request to the Apple DeviceCheck API. For querying, call https://api.devicecheck.apple.com/v1/query_two_bits; for updating, call https://api.devicecheck.apple.com/v1/update_two_bits, including parameters such as device_token, transaction_id, timestamp, bit0, and bit1.

step.name: Step 6: Verify Successful Retrieval or Update of Device Status
step.text: Confirm that the Apple server returns HTTP status code 200. When querying, parse the returned JSON for bit0 and bit1 values; when updating, no content is returned but status code 200 indicates success, meaning the DeviceCheck status has been correctly read or written.

c3150cdc85dd.name: How to Set Up and Automate Xiaomi Smart Home Devices with Apple HomeKit
c3150cdc85dd.description: This tutorial introduces how to connect Xiaomi smart cameras and Xiaomi smart desk lamps through Apple HomeKit and third-party HomeBridge, and how to set up scenes and automation.

step.name: Step 1: Install and Open the Apple Home App
step.text: Open the App Store on your iPhone or iPad, search for and install the "Home" app. After installation, open the Home app and prepare to add the HomeKit-supported Xiaomi Desk Lamp Pro.

step.name: Step 2: Scan the HomeKit QR Code on the Bottom of the Xiaomi Desk Lamp Pro to Add Accessory
step.text: In the Home app, tap the "+" button at the top right corner, select "Add Accessory," and use the camera to scan the HomeKit QR code on the bottom of the Xiaomi Desk Lamp Pro to complete adding the accessory.

step.name: Step 3: Install Node.js and Confirm npm Installation
step.text: Download and install the Node.js 12.6.0 installer from the official Node.js website. After installation, open the Mac terminal and enter the command sudo npm -v. If a version number is displayed, npm has been successfully installed.

step.name: Step 4: Install HomeBridge and Related Plugins
step.text: In the terminal, enter sudo npm -g install homebridge --unsafe-perm to install HomeBridge. Then, depending on your device needs, install the Xiaomi camera plugin with sudo npm install -g homebridge-mijia-camera, or the Xiaomi LED smart desk lamp plugin with sudo npm install -g homebridge-yeelight-wifi.

step.name: Step 5: Obtain Xiaomi Camera IP and Token, and Edit HomeBridge Configuration File
step.text: Open the Xiaomi app, go to the camera settings page to get the IP address. Use iTunes to back up your phone data, then export the digital _mihome.sqlite file with iBackupViewer. Use sqlite-viewer to view and copy the ZTOKEN, then decrypt it using aes.online-domain-tools.com to get the final Token. Edit ~/.homebridge/config.json, enter the IP and Token to complete the configuration.

step.name: Step 6: Start HomeBridge and Add Non-HomeKit Devices to the Home App
step.text: In the terminal, enter sudo homebridge start to start HomeBridge, or sudo homebridge restart to restart it. After starting, a HomeKit QR code will be displayed. Use the Home app to scan and add the accessory, enabling control of Xiaomi devices that originally do not support HomeKit.

step.name: Step 7: Set Up Scenes and Automation in the Home App
step.text: Open the Home app, tap the "+" at the top right corner to add a new scene, enter a name and add the connected accessories. Set the accessory states to complete the scene setup. Switch to the "Automation" tab to add automation scripts, setting device states for arriving home or leaving home. You can use third-party apps like myHome Plus or IFTTT to trigger automation via GPS.

step.name: Step 8: Verify Successful Addition and Control of Xiaomi Smart Home Devices
step.text: Confirm in the Home app that all Xiaomi devices have been successfully added. Use scenes or automation functions to control the camera and desk lamp power. If you can adjust brightness, color, and receive automation notifications normally, the setup is successful.

a66ce3dc8bb9.name: How to Install Apple Watch Catalyst and Muvit Protective Cases
a66ce3dc8bb9.description: This tutorial explains how to remove the Apple Watch band and install the Catalyst and Muvit protective cases, including disassembly, assembly, and installation steps, as well as verifying the functionality after installation.

step.name: Step 1: Unscrew the back screws of the Catalyst protective case
step.text: Use the included small screwdriver to unscrew the screws on the back of the Catalyst protective case. Make sure the screws are fully loosened to allow removal of the case.

step.name: Step 2: Push out the Catalyst protective case body
step.text: Hold the watch band with both hands and use your thumbs to push outward, sliding the Catalyst protective case body out from the band to complete the case removal.

step.name: Step 3: Remove the Apple Watch from the original band
step.text: Flip the Apple Watch to its back, press the rectangular release buttons on the top and bottom with your fingernails, then slide the band left or right to detach the Apple Watch from the original sport band.

step.name: Step 4: Place the Apple Watch into the Catalyst waterproof case
step.text: Carefully place the Apple Watch body into the Catalyst waterproof case, ensuring there are no wrinkles on the case to avoid affecting its waterproof performance.

step.name: Step 5: Attach the Catalyst protective case cover and reassemble the band
step.text: Fit the protective case cover onto the case, avoiding wrinkles, then snap the band body back in place. Use the screwdriver to tighten the screws, being careful not to overtighten.

step.name: Step 6: Remove the Apple Watch from the original band and insert it into the Muvit protective case
step.text: Flip the Apple Watch to its back, press the rectangular release buttons on the top and bottom with your fingernails, slide the band left or right to remove the Apple Watch from the original sport band, then place the Apple Watch face down into the Muvit protective case.

step.name: Step 7: Reattach the band to complete the Muvit protective case installation
step.text: Attach the band back onto the Apple Watch to complete the installation of the Muvit protective case.

step.name: Step 8: Verify the protective case installation is successful
step.text: Confirm that the Apple Watch charges normally, heart rate detection works properly, the screen is unobstructed, the Digital Crown operates smoothly, and audio recording is normal, indicating the protective case installation is successful.

46410aaada00.name: How to Use mitmproxy for Man-in-the-Middle Attack to Sniff API Data on iOS and MacOS
46410aaada00.description: This tutorial demonstrates how to install mitmproxy on MacOS, configure the phone proxy server and install a custom certificate, use mitmproxy to sniff API data from iOS apps, and finally verify if the data interception was successful.

step.name: Step 1: Install mitmproxy Using Homebrew
step.text: Enter the command brew install mitmproxy in the MacOS terminal to install. If you see brew: command not found, please install Homebrew first with the command /usr/bin/ruby -e "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/master/install)".

step.name: Step 2: Start the mitmproxy Service
step.text: After installation, start the service by entering the mitmproxy command in the terminal. If a startup success message appears, the service has started successfully.

step.name: Step 3: Connect Phone and Mac to the Same Local Network and Obtain Mac IP Address
step.text: Ensure the Mac and phone are connected to the same WiFi. On MacOS, go to System Preferences -> Network -> Wi-Fi to check the IP address. If the Mac uses a wired connection, open System Preferences -> Sharing -> Ethernet -> Wi-Fi and enable Internet Sharing. The IP address will be the hotspot's IP, such as 192.168.2.1.

step.name: Step 4: Configure HTTP Proxy Server in Phone WiFi Settings
step.text: On the phone, go to Settings -> WiFi, select the current network, tap HTTP Proxy, set it to Manual, enter the Mac's IP address in the Server field, enter 8080 for the port, and save the settings.

step.name: Step 5: Install mitmproxy Custom Certificate on Phone Safari Browser
step.text: Use the phone's Safari to open http://mitm.it, follow the instructions to download and install the mitmproxy certificate profile. After installation, go to Settings -> General -> About -> Certificate Trust Settings on the phone and enable trust for the mitmproxy certificate.

step.name: Step 6: Use mitmproxy to Observe and Sniff API Data from Phone Apps
step.text: Return to the mitmproxy window in the MacOS terminal, clear the logs (press z), open the target app and trigger API requests (such as pull to refresh). In the mitmproxy interface, find the corresponding request record, press enter to view Request and Response content, and you can use e to edit the request or r to resend it.

step.name: Step 7: Verify Successful Interception of API Data
step.text: If the mitmproxy terminal displays the phone app's API requests and responses, and the phone browser can browse web pages normally, the man-in-the-middle sniffing is successful. If certificate errors or connection failures occur, please verify that the certificate installation and proxy settings are correct.

21119db777dd.name: How to Use iOS Shortcuts with Mi Home Smart Home Automation
21119db777dd.description: A guide on how to use the built-in Shortcuts app on iOS 13.1 and above with Mi Home smart devices to set up automation for leaving and arriving home.

step.name: Step 1: Open the Mi Home App and Set Up Smart Actions
step.text: Open the Mi Home app on your phone, switch to the "Me" tab and then to the "Smart" section. Confirm that your smart devices have been added to Mi Home, then select "Manual Execution." Pull down the "Smart Devices" section to add the devices and actions you want to operate. After completing, tap "Save" at the top right and enter a name for this smart action.

step.name: Step 2: Add Smart Action to Siri Shortcuts
step.text: In the Mi Home app's smart action list, tap "Add to Siri" next to the smart action you want to add. Enter the phrase you will use to call Siri, then tap "Add to Siri" to complete the setup. Note that the phrase must not conflict with any built-in iOS commands.

step.name: Step 3: Open the Shortcuts App to Set Up Automation
step.text: Open the built-in Shortcuts app on iOS, switch to the "Automation" tab, tap the "+" button at the top right to add a new automation, select "Create Personal Automation," set the type to "Arrive" or "Leave," and specify the corresponding location. After finishing, tap "Next" at the top right.

step.name: Step 4: Add the Run Shortcut Action
step.text: In the automation setup, tap "Add Action," choose the "Scripting" category, scroll to the "Shortcuts" section, select "Run Shortcut," then tap the "Shortcut" field and choose the Siri shortcut you previously added in the Mi Home app. Finally, tap "Done" at the top right to save the automation settings.

step.name: Step 5: Verify if the Automation is Successful
step.text: When your phone or Apple Watch enters or leaves the set location range, you will receive a notification that the shortcut is running. Tap the notification to execute the configured Mi Home smart action. If you successfully receive the notification and the action executes, the automation setup is successful.

b08ef940c196.name: How to Implement Deferred Deep Link on iOS
b08ef940c196.description: This article explains how to implement Deferred Deep Link on iOS using Swift, allowing users to retain source data after installing the app. It includes detailed steps for using the clipboard method on iOS 10 and above, and the Cookie sharing method on iOS 9.

step.name: Step 1: Copy deep link information to clipboard using clipboard.js on the web
step.text: Use the clipboard.js package on the webpage. When the user clicks the "Go Now" button, copy the information to be passed to the app (e.g., marry://topicID=1&type=topic) to the clipboard, then use location.href to redirect to the App Store page.

step.name: Step 2: Read clipboard and parse URL Scheme on the app side
step.text: In AppDelegate or the main UIViewController, use Swift to read the clipboard content. Parse the clipboard string with URL(string:), check if the scheme is the default marry, and use a custom queryParameters extension method to parse parameters. Display the corresponding screen based on the parameters.

step.name: Step 3: Clear deep link information from the clipboard
step.text: After processing the deep link information in the clipboard, clear the clipboard content by setting UIPasteboard.general.string = "" to avoid triggering the same deep link repeatedly.

step.name: Step 4: Use Cookie sharing method to store deep link information on iOS 9
step.text: On the webpage, use JavaScript to store deep link information in a Cookie. Use the setcookie(name, val, day) method to set the Cookie with a default expiration of one day, then redirect to the App Store page.

step.name: Step 5: Load SFSafariViewController in the app background to read Cookie
step.text: In the main UIViewController's viewDidLoad, initialize SFSafariViewController and add its view to a transparent and visible UIView. Load the loadCookie.html page from the same domain to read the Cookie, and set SFSafariViewControllerDelegate to listen for the load completion event.

step.name: Step 6: Read Cookie via loadCookie.html page and trigger URL Scheme
step.text: The loadCookie.html page executes the JavaScript function checkCookie() on body onload, reads the Cookie named iOSDeepLinkData, and if it exists, clears the Cookie and sets window.location.href to the URL Scheme contained in the Cookie, triggering the app's URL Scheme handling process.

step.name: Step 7: Verify if Deferred Deep Link was successfully triggered
step.text: After installing and opening the app, confirm that the app can read deep link information from the clipboard or Cookie and automatically navigate to the corresponding target page, such as TopicViewController, indicating successful implementation of Deferred Deep Link.

14cee137c565.name: How to Create iOS UIViewController Pull-Down Dismiss and Full-Page Right-Swipe Back Animation Effects
14cee137c565.description: This tutorial demonstrates how to customize pull-down dismiss and full-page right-swipe back animations for UIViewController in iOS, including gesture interaction control and transition animation implementation.

step.name: Step 1: Set UIViewController's transitioningDelegate and modalPresentationStyle
step.text: In the UIViewController to be presented, set modalPresentationStyle to .custom, and assign the transitioningDelegate to the UIViewController itself or another object responsible for handling the transition, ensuring the custom animation is triggered.

step.name: Step 2: Implement UIViewControllerAnimatedTransitioning protocol to define transition animations
step.text: Create a class that implements UIViewControllerAnimatedTransitioning, implementing the transitionDuration and animateTransition methods. Use UIView.animate to control the view's appearance and disappearance animations, such as adjusting view position and opacity, and call transitionContext.completeTransition when the animation finishes.

step.name: Step 3: Implement UIPercentDrivenInteractiveTransition and bind gesture control
step.text: Create a class inheriting from UIPercentDrivenInteractiveTransition, and bind a UIPanGestureRecognizer to the specified interactive view during initialization. Calculate the swipe percentage from gesture events, and call update, finish, or cancel methods to control animation progress and completion state.

step.name: Step 4: Return custom animation and interaction controller objects in transitioningDelegate
step.text: In the UIViewControllerTransitioningDelegate methods, return the custom UIViewControllerAnimatedTransitioning object to define the animation, and return the UIPercentDrivenInteractiveTransition object in interactionControllerForDismissal or interactionControllerForPresentation to enable gesture interaction control.

step.name: Step 5: Verify successful implementation of pull-down dismiss and full-page right-swipe back animations
step.text: Run the app, tap the button that triggers Present, try to pull down with a gesture to dismiss the page, or use the full-page right-swipe gesture in UINavigationController to go back to the previous page. Confirm the animation is smooth and gesture control works properly, indicating successful custom transition animation and interaction control.

a8c2d7ed144b.name: How to Expand UIButton Tap Area on iOS
a8c2d7ed144b.description: Learn how to expand the tap area of a UIButton by customizing the pointInside method, enhancing user experience.

step.name: Step 1: Customize UIButton class and add touchEdgeInsets property
step.text: Create a custom class MyButton that inherits from UIButton, and add a public touchEdgeInsets property to set the boundary distances for expanding the tap area.

step.name: Step 2: Override pointInside method to expand touch area
step.text: Override the point(inside:with:) method in the MyButton class, use touchEdgeInsets to adjust the original bounds, expanding the touch area, and return whether the tap coordinate is within the expanded area.

step.name: Step 3: Set MyButton's touchEdgeInsets in UIViewController
step.text: In the UIViewController using MyButton, set playerButton.touchEdgeInsets to UIEdgeInsets(top: -10, left: -10, bottom: -10, right: -10) inside the viewDidLoad method to expand the tap area outward by 10 points.

step.name: Step 4: Verify if tap area expansion is successful
step.text: Run the app, tap the MyButton with touchEdgeInsets set, confirm that the button's tap area is larger than the original bounds and the tap event triggers correctly, indicating successful tap area expansion.

7498e1ff93ce.name: How to Extract and Dump iOS APP ipa Files Using a Jailbroken Phone
7498e1ff93ce.description: This tutorial demonstrates how to prepare the environment and use the frida-ios-dump tool with a jailbroken phone to extract and dump iOS APPs downloaded from the App Store, obtaining analyzable original ipa files.

step.name: Step 1: Install Python and pip on Mac
step.text: Ensure Python 2.7 or Python 3.X is installed on your Mac. If using Python 3, install it yourself. Then install the pip package manager to manage Python packages using the pip command.

step.name: Step 2: Install the frida package
step.text: In Terminal, enter the command sudo pip3 install frida --upgrade --ignore-installed six (if using Python 3) to install the frida package. After installation, run the frida-ps command. If no error messages appear, the installation was successful.

step.name: Step 3: Clone the frida-ios-dump project and verify SSH settings
step.text: Use git clone https://github.com/AloneMonkey/frida-ios-dump to download the project. Enter the project directory and open dump.py with a text editor. Confirm the SSH connection settings are User='root', Password='alpine', Host='localhost', Port=2222.

step.name: Step 4: Install Open SSH and Frida on the jailbroken phone
step.text: Open Cydia on the jailbroken phone, search for and install Open SSH. Then add the Frida repository https://build.frida.re and install the corresponding Frida package based on your phone's processor version, for example, iPhone 6 is a pre-A12 version.

step.name: Step 5: Use iproxy to create a local SSH tunnel to the phone
step.text: Connect the jailbroken phone to the Mac via USB. Open Terminal and enter iproxy 2222 22 to start forwarding local port 2222 to the phone's port 22, preparing for SSH connection.

step.name: Step 6: Connect to the jailbroken phone via SSH
step.text: In another Terminal window, enter ssh root@127.0.0.1. When prompted, enter the default password alpine. Once connected successfully, you can remotely operate the phone.

step.name: Step 7: Use dump.py to list installed apps on the phone
step.text: Switch to the frida-ios-dump project directory and run python3 dump.py -l to list all installed or running app names and Bundle IDs on the phone.

step.name: Step 8: Use dump.py to dump and export ipa files
step.text: Run python3 dump.py APPNameOrBundleID -o output_path/filename.ipa, specifying the output path to avoid permission issues. Upon success, a dumped ipa file will be generated.

step.name: Step 9: Verify successful retrieval of the ipa file
step.text: Rename the exported ipa file as a zip file, right-click to unzip it, and check if the /Payload/APPName.app directory exists. If it does, the original app file has been successfully obtained.

99db2a1fbfe5.name: How to Set Up HomeBridge on Raspberry Pi and Integrate Mijia Smart Appliances into HomeKit
99db2a1fbfe5.description: This tutorial demonstrates how to set up the Raspberry Pi environment from scratch, install HomeBridge, and connect Mijia smart appliances to Apple HomeKit through HomeBridge to achieve smart home control.

step.name: Step 1: Flash Raspberry Pi OS to micro SD Card
step.text: Insert the micro SD card into the card reader and connect it to the computer, then open the Etcher software. First, select the downloaded Raspberry Pi OS image file (xxxx.img), second, select the memory card device, and click "Flash!" to start flashing. Enter the MacOS password and wait for the flashing and verification to complete. If an error occurs, format the memory card and try again.

step.name: Step 2: Enable SSH and Connect to Raspberry Pi
step.text: After flashing is complete, reinsert the memory card into the computer, and create a blank file named "ssh" without any extension in the root directory of the memory card. Insert the memory card into the Raspberry Pi and connect the network cable and power supply. After booting, use Mac Terminal to enter ssh pi@RaspberryPiIP (e.g., 192.168.0.110). For the first connection, enter yes, the password is the default raspberry. Once connected successfully, you can operate the Raspberry Pi.

step.name: Step 3: Install and Configure Basic Tools and Node.js Environment on Raspberry Pi
step.text: In the Raspberry Pi Terminal, enter sudo apt-get install vim to install the editor. To resolve locale warnings, use vim to edit the .bashrc file, add export LC_ALL=C, save and execute source .bashrc. Then install the nvm management tool by running curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/creationix/nvm/v0.33.11/install.sh | bash, and install Node.js version 12.16.2 using nvm install 12.16.2. After confirming installation, use which node to check the node path, and create a link with sudo ln -fs [path] /usr/local/bin/node.

step.name: Step 4: Enable Raspberry Pi VNC Remote Desktop and Set to Auto Start on Boot
step.text: Run sudo raspi-config, select Interfacing Options -> P3 VNC, and choose Yes to enable the VNC remote desktop feature. Use vim to edit /etc/init.d/vncserver, paste the specified script content and save. Then execute sudo chmod 755 /etc/init.d/vncserver and sudo update-rc.d vncserver defaults. After completion, enter sudo reboot to restart the Raspberry Pi.

step.name: Step 5: Install HomeBridge and Set to Auto Start on Boot
step.text: In Terminal, enter npm -g install homebridge - unsafe-perm to install HomeBridge (without sudo). Create or modify config.json in the /home/pi/.homebridge directory, paste the basic configuration content and save. Use homebridge start to launch the service, and scan the QRCode with the iPhone Home app to add the accessory. Use which homebridge to find the homebridge path, edit /etc/init.d/homebridge, paste the specified script and replace the path, save and then run chmod 755 and update-rc.d commands to complete the auto start on boot setup.

step.name: Step 6: Obtain Mijia Smart Appliance Information and Integrate into HomeBridge
step.text: First, add Mijia smart appliances to the Mijia app. Connect the iPhone to the Mac, back up the phone data locally, and use iBackupViewer to read the backup. Locate the digital _mihome.sqlite file and export it. Use an online sqlite viewer to query the ZDID, ZNAME, and ZTOKEN fields. Convert the ZTOKEN to Token via the specified AES decryption website and record the device IP.

step.name: Step 7: Install Corresponding HomeBridge Plugins and Modify config.json
step.text: Depending on the Mijia device, enter npm install -g corresponding homebridge plugins in Terminal (such as homebridge-mijia-camera, homebridge-mi-fan, homebridge-xiaomi-air-purifier3, homebridge-yeelight-wifi). Modify config.json by adding accessory or platform settings into the accessories or platforms section, filling in device IP, Token, ZDID, and other information. Save and restart the HomeBridge service, then scan with the Home app to add the accessory.

step.name: Step 8: Verify Successful Integration of HomeBridge and Mijia Appliances
step.text: After starting HomeBridge, open the iPhone Home app and confirm that all Mijia appliance accessories are displayed and controllable properly (such as camera on/off, fan speed adjustment, air purifier on/off and status, smart lighting adjustments, etc.). If functions work normally without error messages, the integration is successful.

2e4429f410d6.name: How to Create a Fake Perspective Transparent Phone Wallpaper Using iPhone
2e4429f410d6.description: Use the built-in iMovie app on iPhone and a green background to shoot and composite videos, creating a perspective transparent phone wallpaper effect.

step.name: Step 1: Set up the shooting phone
step.text: Use a phone stand or other supports (such as two eel cans and a bottle of mineral water) to fix the shooting phone, ensuring the shooting angle is stable and consistent to avoid frame shifts.

step.name: Step 2: Shoot a clean background video
step.text: Use the set-up phone to shoot a clean video without the phone appearing in the frame. The video length can be adjusted as needed, ensuring the frame is clean and the angle is fixed.

step.name: Step 3: Set the phone wallpaper to the green background image
step.text: Set the phone wallpaper that will appear in the frame to the green background image by going to 'Settings' -> 'Wallpaper' -> select the downloaded green background image, and set it as the wallpaper.

step.name: Step 4: Shoot the phone operation video
step.text: Use the shooting phone to record a video of the phone operation appearing in the frame. The video length should be the same or longer than the clean video and can be trimmed later in iMovie.

step.name: Step 5: Composite the videos using iMovie
step.text: Open the iMovie app, create a new project, add the clean video first and move the playhead to the beginning. Then insert the phone operation video, select it, click the 'Green/Blue Screen' feature, and tap the green area to complete the chroma keying.

step.name: Step 6: Export the composited video
step.text: After confirming that the end times of both videos match, click 'Done' at the top left, then click 'Share' below to select the export destination and complete the video export.

step.name: Step 7: Verify the success
step.text: Play the exported video to confirm that the green background of the phone appearing in the frame has been keyed out, showing the perspective transparent phone wallpaper effect, indicating successful creation.

1aa2f8445642.name: How to Solve JSON Decode Issues Using Swift Codable
1aa2f8445642.description: An introduction to using Swift Codable (Decodable) protocol and JSONDecoder from basic to advanced scenarios for JSON parsing, including custom decoding, generic wrappers, date format handling, polymorphic data decoding, skipping erroneous data, and other techniques.

step.name: Step 1: Define Entity and Model Structures
step.text: First, define the Entity structure corresponding to JSON fields, implement the Decodable protocol, and separate the Model class responsible for business logic and internal fields. Initialize the Model with the Entity to ensure clear responsibility separation and Objective-C compatibility.

step.name: Step 2: Use JSONDecoder to Parse JSON String
step.text: Create an instance of JSONDecoder, convert the JSON string to Data, use the decode method to parse it into the corresponding Entity structure, and use do-catch to capture parsing errors.

step.name: Step 3: Customize init(from decoder: Decoder) to Handle Complex Decoding Logic
step.text: Implement init(from decoder: Decoder) in the Entity, use container(keyedBy:), singleValueContainer(), or unkeyedContainer() to obtain data containers, decode fields as needed, and use nestedContainer to handle nested data.

step.name: Step 4: Handle Polymorphic Data and Conditional Decoding
step.text: For fields that may have multiple types, use Associated Value Enum or Base Class approach. First read key fields to determine the type, then decode into different Entities based on the type, and perform type casting when using them.

step.name: Step 5: Use decodeIfPresent to Skip Optional Fields
step.text: For fields that may be missing in JSON, use decodeIfPresent to decode them. If the field does not exist, provide a default value to avoid decoding failure.

step.name: Step 6: Skip Data That Fails to Decode in Arrays
step.text: When decoding arrays, use nestedUnkeyedContainer combined with try? to attempt decoding each item. Use an empty Decodable struct to continue iterating when decoding fails, preventing the entire array from failing to decode.

step.name: Step 7: Verify Successful JSON Parsing and Use Model
step.text: After parsing the JSON string with JSONDecoder, convert the result into a Model object, and verify data correctness through the Model's properties and methods, such as printing comment content. If no errors occur, it indicates success.

724a7fb9a364.name: How to Bind a Personal Domain to Google Site
724a7fb9a364.description: This tutorial explains how to bind a personal domain to a Google Site website, including DNS settings and Google Site configuration steps.

step.name: Step 1: Go to Google Domain Verification Page to Add Resource
step.text: Open your browser and go to http://google.com/webmasters/verification, click "Add Resource" and enter your domain name, then press the "Continue" button.

step.name: Step 2: Copy the DNS Verification String
step.text: On the verification page, select your domain service provider. The system will display the DNS verification string. Please copy this string for later use.

step.name: Step 3: Add TXT and CNAME Records on Domain Service Provider Website
step.text: Log in to your domain service provider website (e.g., Namecheap.com), go to the DNS settings section, add a TXT Record with the host field as "@" and paste the copied DNS verification string into the value field, then submit. Next, add a CNAME Record with the host field as "www" or your desired subdomain, and the value field as "ghs.googlehosted.com.", then submit.

step.name: Step 4: Return to Google Domain Verification Page and Click Verify
step.text: After waiting for the DNS records to take effect, return to the Google domain verification page and click the "Verify" button. If verification fails, please wait a while and try again. If it still cannot verify after more than an hour, check if the DNS settings are correct.

step.name: Step 5: Set Custom URL and Publish on Google Site
step.text: Open your Google Site settings page, click the gear icon at the top right and select "Custom URL", enter the domain name or subdomain you want to assign, then click "Assign". After successful assignment, close the settings window and click the "Publish" button at the top right to publish the site. Wait for the DNS records to take effect.

step.name: Step 6: Verify if the Domain is Successfully Bound
step.text: Open a new browser window, enter the domain URL you just set, and confirm that the website opens properly. If the page cannot be opened, please wait a while and try again, and check if the DNS settings are correct.

41c49a75a743.name: How to Write and Configure Run Script in Xcode Using Swift for Multilingual and Image Resource Checks
41c49a75a743.description: A guide on how to download and configure the multilingual check tool Localize and the image resource check tool Asset Checker, both written in Swift, and add them to Xcode Build Phases as Run Script. Finally, compile them into executables to improve build efficiency.

step.name: Step 1: Download and Place the Localize.swift File
step.text: Download the Localize.swift file from the Localize project's GitHub page and place it in the project directory, for example at the path ${SRCROOT}/Localize.swift.

step.name: Step 2: Add a Run Script Phase in Xcode and Set the Script Path
step.text: Open the Xcode project settings, select the iOS Target, go to Build Phases, click the '+' button at the top left to add a New Run Script Phase, and paste the script path in the Script field, for example ${SRCROOT}/Localize.swift.

step.name: Step 3: Edit Localize.swift for Custom Configuration
step.text: Open Localize.swift in Xcode and modify the parameters in the upper configuration block, such as enabling the check script by setting enabled to true, setting the language file directories relativeLocalizableFolders, project directory relativeSourceFolder, regular expression patterns, and other check options.

step.name: Step 4: Build the Project and Verify Localize Check Results
step.text: Build the project in Xcode. Localize will automatically check for missing, duplicate, unused, and untranslated multilingual files and display error or warning messages in the build output.

step.name: Step 5: Download and Place the AssetChecker.swift File
step.text: Download the AssetChecker.swift file from the Asset Checker project's GitHub page and place it in the project directory, for example at the path ${SRCROOT}/AssetChecker.swift.

step.name: Step 6: Add a Run Script Phase in Xcode and Set Asset Checker Script and Parameters
step.text: In the Xcode project settings under Build Phases, add a Run Script Phase and paste a command like ${SRCROOT}/AssetChecker.swift ${SRCROOT}/project_directory ${SRCROOT}/Resources/Images.xcassets in the Script field, setting the project directory and image resource directory paths.

step.name: Step 7: Build the Project and Verify Asset Checker Results
step.text: Build the project in Xcode. Asset Checker will automatically check for missing and redundant image resources and display error or warning messages in the build output.

step.name: Step 8: Compile Swift Scripts into Executables to Improve Build Speed
step.text: Open the terminal, navigate to the directory containing Localize.swift or AssetChecker.swift, run the command swiftc -o Localize Localize.swift to compile the Swift script into an executable, then change the Run Script path in Xcode Build Phases to the executable path, for example ${SRCROOT}/Localize.

step.name: Step 9: Verify Successful Execution of Automatic Checks
step.text: Build the project in Xcode and confirm that the Run Script in Build Phases executes successfully and outputs the multilingual and image resource check results. No errors indicate that the setup is complete and functioning properly.

6ce488898003.name: How to Implement Local Cache Functionality Using AVPlayer
6ce488898003.description: This tutorial explains how to use AVPlayer, AVURLAsset, and AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate to implement local caching during music playback, including segmented file downloading, cache management, and resource loading processes.

step.name: Step 1: Verify Server Support for HTTP Range Requests
step.text: Use the curl command to send HEAD or GET requests to the music file, check if the Response Header includes Accept-Ranges: bytes, as well as Content-Length and Content-Type, to confirm that the server supports segmented downloading and obtaining the file length.

step.name: Step 2: Customize AVURLAsset and Replace URL Scheme
step.text: Create a CachingAVURLAsset class inheriting from AVURLAsset, change the original URL scheme from http/https to a custom scheme cacheable, and initialize a ResourceLoader object set as AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate, allowing the system to use a custom resource loading process.

step.name: Step 3: Implement ResourceLoaderDelegate to Handle Resource Requests
step.text: Implement the resourceLoader(_:shouldWaitForLoadingOfRequestedResource:) method in ResourceLoader to determine if the request is for file info or data; first read data from local cache and respond, if data is insufficient, initiate a URLSession request to download from the network and write the downloaded data back to the local cache.

step.name: Step 4: Use PINCache or FileHandle to Manage Local Cache Data
step.text: Use PINCache for cache data access, or switch to FileHandle to avoid memory overflow caused by large files; implement the AssetDataManager protocol to support storing file info and segmented data, and to merge continuous cached segments.

step.name: Step 5: Play Music in AVPlayer Using Custom CachingAVURLAsset
step.text: Determine if the URL supports the custom scheme, create a CachingAVURLAsset instance and pass it to AVPlayer to start playback; the system will manage local cache and network download data through ResourceLoader.

step.name: Step 6: Verify Successful Implementation of Local Cache Functionality
step.text: Monitor network requests and local cache data to confirm that music playback reads data in segments from the local cache, reducing repeated downloads; also check the cache file directory in the simulator or device to ensure cache files exist and playback works properly.

948ed34efa09.name: How to Set Up Cross-Platform Account Password Integration for iOS APP
948ed34efa09.description: Learn how to configure Associated Domains in your iOS APP, upload the apple-app-site-association file, and use Password AutoFill and the Shared Web Credentials API to integrate website and APP account passwords, enhancing user login experience.

step.name: Step 1: Add Associated Domains in Xcode Project
step.text: Open Xcode, go to the project's Signing & Capabilities tab, click the "+ Capabilities" button at the top left, select "Associated Domains", and add an entry in the format webcredentials:yourwebsite.com, for example webcredentials:google.com.

step.name: Step 2: Enable Associated Domains in Apple Developer Portal
step.text: Log in to the Apple Developer Portal, go to Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles, select Identifiers, find your project, enable the Associated Domains capability, and confirm your Team ID, which will be used later for website configuration.

step.name: Step 3: Upload apple-app-site-association File to Website Root or .well-known Directory
step.text: Create a file named apple-app-site-association without an extension using a text editor, with content { "webcredentials": { "apps": ["TeamID.BundleId"] } }. Replace TeamID.BundleId with your project information, then upload this file to the website root directory or the /.well-known directory, ensuring it is accessible via https://yourwebsite.com/apple-app-site-association.

step.name: Step 4: Set TextField textContentType Property in APP
step.text: On the login page, set the textContentType of the username input field to .username, the password input field to .password, and if there is a password confirmation field, set it to .newPassword. This will automatically show saved password options above the keyboard for easy autofill.

step.name: Step 5: Use AuthenticationServices Framework to Read Account Password
step.text: In APPs supporting iOS 13 and above, import AuthenticationServices, create an ASAuthorizationPasswordRequest, use ASAuthorizationController to perform the request, implement ASAuthorizationControllerDelegate, and upon successful authorization, obtain ASPasswordCredential to fill the username and password into the corresponding input fields.

step.name: Step 6: Verify Successful Account Password Integration
step.text: Run the APP, go to the login page, tap the username input field, and check if saved password options appear above the keyboard, or tap the "Choose Saved Password" button. Successfully autofilling website account credentials indicates successful integration.

12c5026da33d.name: How to Set Up a Local Universal Links Testing Environment
12c5026da33d.description: A guide on how to use nginx and ngrok on macOS to create a local Universal Links testing environment, allowing real-time verification of apple-app-site-association settings during development.

step.name: Step 1: Install nginx
step.text: Enter the command brew install nginx in the macOS terminal to install nginx. If Homebrew is not installed, first run /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)" to install Homebrew.

step.name: Step 2: Modify the nginx configuration file
step.text: Go to the /usr/local/etc/nginx/ directory, open the nginx.conf file with a text editor, find the root setting under the server block's location /, change the path to your desired directory (e.g., /Users/zhgchgli/Documents), and ensure the listen port is set to 8080.

step.name: Step 3: Start the nginx service
step.text: Enter the nginx command in the terminal to start the service. To stop nginx, enter nginx -s stop. If you modify nginx.conf and need to reload the configuration, enter nginx -s reload.

step.name: Step 4: Create the .well-known directory and place the apple-app-site-association file
step.text: Create a .well-known directory under the nginx root directory, and place the apple-app-site-association file inside it. If you cannot see .well-known, enter defaults write com.apple.finder AppleShowAllFiles TRUE in the terminal, then enter killall Finder to restart Finder and show hidden folders.

step.name: Step 5: Test the local service using a browser
step.text: Open a browser and enter http://localhost:8080/.well-known/apple-app-site-association to confirm the file can be downloaded properly. If you encounter 404 or 403 errors, check if the root directory setting, file location, and file name are correct; apple-app-site-association must not have a file extension.

step.name: Step 6: Install and configure ngrok to expose the local service
step.text: Register and download the ngrok executable from ngrok.com. After unzipping, run ./ngrok authtoken YOUR_TOKEN in the terminal to set the authorization, then run ./ngrok http 8080 to start the service.

step.name: Step 7: Verify the ngrok public URL and test Universal Links
step.text: Enter the ngrok assigned URL in a browser, for example https://ec87f78bec0f.ngrok.io/.well-known/apple-app-site-association, to confirm the file can be downloaded properly. Add this URL to the APP's Associated Domains setting with the ?mode=developer parameter. Rebuild & Run the APP, click the test URL (e.g., https://ec87f78bec0f.ngrok.io/buy/123); if an “Open” prompt appears and the APP opens successfully, the setup is successful.

87090f101b9a.name: How to Set Up Laravel Homestead and phpMyAdmin Environment on Mac
87090f101b9a.description: From downloading and installing Vagrant and VirtualBox, to configuring Homestead, installing phpMyAdmin, setting up the local Hosts file, and finally starting the virtual machine and verifying the environment success.

step.name: Step 1: Download and Install Vagrant and VirtualBox
step.text: Go to the Vagrant official website https://www.vagrantup.com/downloads to download and install Vagrant, then visit the VirtualBox official website https://www.virtualbox.org/wiki/Downloads to download and install VirtualBox. If the system requires a reboot during VirtualBox installation, follow the instructions to complete it, and go to "Settings" -> "Security & Privacy" to allow VirtualBox to enable all services.

step.name: Step 2: Configure Laravel Homestead Environment
step.text: In the terminal, enter git clone https://github.com/laravel/homestead.git ~/Homestead, then change directory with cd ~/Homestead, use git checkout release to switch to the release branch, and finally run bash init.sh to initialize the Homestead environment.

step.name: Step 3: Download and Configure phpMyAdmin
step.text: Go to the phpMyAdmin official website https://www.phpmyadmin.net/ to download the latest version of phpMyAdmin. After extracting, rename the folder to phpMyAdmin and move this folder into the ~/Homestead directory. Inside the phpMyAdmin folder, find config.sample.inc.php, copy and rename it to config.inc.php. Open it with an editor and modify the following three settings: change auth_type to 'config', user to 'homestead', and password to 'secret'.

step.name: Step 4: Modify Homestead.yaml Configuration File
step.text: Open ~/Homestead/Homestead.yaml with an editor, confirm the ip is set to '192.168.10.10' and provider is 'virtualbox'. In the folders section, add a mapping: map: ~/Homestead/phpMyAdmin, to: /home/vagrant/phpMyAdmin. In the sites section, add a mapping: map: phpMyAdmin.test, to: /home/vagrant/phpMyAdmin.

step.name: Step 5: Modify Local Hosts File to Add Virtual Domain
step.text: Using Finder, select Go -> /etc/hosts, copy the hosts file to the desktop and open it. Add a new line: 192.168.10.10 phpMyAdmin.test. Save and then cut and paste the file back to /etc/hosts to overwrite it, completing the local virtual domain mapping setup.

step.name: Step 6: Start Homestead Virtual Machine and Log In
step.text: In the terminal, switch to the ~/Homestead directory and run the command vagrant up --provision to start the virtual machine and apply the settings. After startup is complete, enter vagrant ssh to log into the virtual machine.

step.name: Step 7: Verify phpMyAdmin Connection Success
step.text: In the browser, enter http://phpmyadmin.test/ to access the phpMyAdmin web interface. If it opens normally and shows the login screen, the environment setup is successful.

70a1409b149a.name: How to Use Python with Google Cloud Platform and Line Bot to Automate Routine Tasks
70a1409b149a.description: Using a check-in reward app as an example, this guide teaches you how to sniff APIs with Proxyman, write a Python script to simulate check-ins, deploy the script to Google Cloud Function, set up Google Cloud Scheduler for automatic execution, and finally send execution result notifications via Line Bot.

step.name: Step 1: Use Proxyman to Sniff APIs
step.text: Go to https://proxyman.io/ to download and install Proxyman. After launching, install the Root certificate for Mac and iOS devices. Set up the phone proxy server and enable the certificate, then open the target app. Proxyman will intercept and unpack HTTPS traffic, allowing you to observe the API requests and parameters during check-in.

step.name: Step 2: Write a Python Script to Simulate Check-in Requests
step.text: Use the requests package to write a Python script that simulates the POST request to the check-in API, including the necessary headers and data captured during sniffing. Add functionality to send messages via Line Bot by obtaining the User ID and Channel Access Token from the Line Developers Console, and use HTTP POST in the script to call the Line API to send notifications.

step.name: Step 3: Deploy the Python Script to Google Cloud Function
step.text: Log in to Google Cloud Console, create a project, and enable Cloud Functions. Create a new function, select the Python 3.x runtime environment, set the trigger to HTTP, and allow unauthenticated calls. Upload the Python script and requirements.txt (including requests and firebase-admin). After deployment, obtain the trigger URL.

step.name: Step 4: Configure Google Cloud Scheduler for Automatic Execution
step.text: Go to the Google Cloud Scheduler console, create a new job, set the schedule time (e.g., daily at 1:15 AM, crontab format: 15 1 * * *), set the HTTP target to the Cloud Function trigger URL, choose GET as the HTTP method, and select Taiwan as the time zone. After creation, you can run an immediate test to confirm the schedule triggers correctly.

step.name: Step 5: Verify Whether Automatic Check-in is Successful
step.text: Receive execution result notifications via Line Bot. If you receive the message "CheckIn Success!", it means the check-in script executed successfully. You can also check the Cloud Function execution logs and Scheduler execution status in Google Cloud Console to confirm no errors occurred.

d9a95d4224ea.name: How to Set Up a Custom Domain on Medium
d9a95d4224ea.description: This tutorial explains how to purchase a domain and set up the custom domain feature on Medium, including the Namecheap purchase process and DNS configuration steps, and finally how to verify if the setup is successful.

step.name: Step 1: Search and Purchase a Domain on Namecheap
step.text: Go to the Namecheap homepage (https://namecheap.pxf.io/P0jdZQ), enter your desired domain name in the search bar. If the button on the right shows "Add To Cart," it means the domain is available for purchase. Click to add it to your cart, then click "Checkout" below to proceed to the payment page. Choose whether to enable auto-renewal and select the number of years to purchase. Enter your credit card information and click "Confirm Order" to complete the purchase.

step.name: Step 2: Log in to Namecheap and Access the Domain Management Page
step.text: After logging into your Namecheap account, click the account icon in the top left corner and select "Dashboard." Switch to the "Domain List" tab, find the domain you just purchased, and click the "Manage" button on the right to enter the domain management page.

step.name: Step 3: Go to the Advanced DNS Settings Page
step.text: In the domain management page, switch to the rightmost "Advanced DNS" tab to prepare for DNS host record configuration.

step.name: Step 4: Set Up a Custom Domain on Medium and Obtain DNS Information
step.text: Go to the Medium settings page (https://medium.com/me/settings), find the "Custom domain" section in the Profile block, and click "Get started." If "Upgrade" appears, you need to upgrade to a paid membership first. Enter your domain name (e.g., www.example.com), and the system will display the DNS record information you need to configure. Please note this information.

step.name: Step 5: Return to Namecheap to Add DNS Host Records
step.text: Go back to Namecheap's "Advanced DNS" tab, find the "HOST RECORDS" section, and click "ADD NEW RECORD" twice to add two records. Enter the DNS record information provided by Medium, select "A Record" as the type, enter the subdomain name (such as www or your custom subdomain) as the host name, and fill in the IP address specified by Medium. After completing, click the checkmark on the right to save.

step.name: Step 6: Return to Medium to Complete Setup and Wait for Propagation
step.text: After completing the settings on Namecheap, return to the Medium settings page and click "Continue" to proceed. The system will display a processing page, indicating the setup has been submitted. DNS propagation can take up to 48 hours, but it may take as little as 15 minutes to take effect.

step.name: Step 7: Verify if the Custom Domain Is Successfully Active
step.text: Use a browser to enter your custom domain (e.g., blog.zhgchg.li). If it correctly redirects to the Medium page, the setup is successful. If a 404 page appears, it means the DNS has not yet propagated. Please wait up to 48 hours and check again.

99a6cef90190.name: How to Write a Python Script for Brute-Forcing SMS Verification Codes
99a6cef90190.description: Demonstrates how to write a multithreaded Python script to brute-force SMS verification codes and verify the cracking results.

step.name: Step 1: Sniff the Verification Request API
step.text: Use Proxyman or the Network panel in Chrome Developer Tools to monitor the API requests sent after submitting the SMS verification code. Obtain the POST request URL and parameter format for verification, such as POST https://zhgchg.li/findPWD, with parameters including phone and code.

step.name: Step 2: Write the Python Brute-Force Script
step.text: Create crack.py and use the requests module to send POST requests to the verification API. Use the threading module to group attempts for four-digit codes from 0000 to 9999. Determine if the code is correct based on the response, print the correct code upon success, and stop other threads.

step.name: Step 3: Run the Script and Observe Results
step.text: Run python crack.py in the terminal. The script will launch multiple threads simultaneously to try different code ranges. When the correct code (e.g., 1743) is found, it will output the result in the terminal and stop further attempts.

step.name: Step 4: Verify Successful Cracking
step.text: Use the cracked verification code (e.g., 1743) on the password reset page or login process to confirm that you can successfully reset the password or log in, indicating the brute-force verification code process was successful.

9659db1357e4.name: How to Quickly Build a Testable API Service Using Firebase Firestore and Functions
9659db1357e4.description: This tutorial demonstrates how to install the Node.js environment locally, deploy Firebase Functions, and write and test RESTful APIs, including adding, modifying, deleting, querying articles, and like counter features, followed by stress testing for validation.

step.name: Step 1: Install the Node.js environment
step.text: Use NVM to install Node.js. First, copy the installation shell script from the NVM GitHub project and run the command curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.37.2/install.sh | bash. If an error occurs, ensure that the home directory contains a ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc file; if not, create one before running the installation again. After installation, run nvm install node to install the latest Node.js version, and finally verify the installation with npm --version.

step.name: Step 2: Install and log in to Firebase CLI
step.text: Globally install Firebase CLI using npm by running npm install -g firebase-tools. After installation, execute firebase login to authenticate your Firebase account and ensure you can manage Firebase projects.

step.name: Step 3: Initialize Firebase project and configure Functions
step.text: In the folder where you want to create the project, run firebase init and select Functions (and Firestore) features. Set the language to JavaScript, choose whether to enable ESLint, and agree to use npm to install dependencies. After completion, a functions folder and related configuration files will be generated.

step.name: Step 4: Write and deploy Firebase Functions code
