fd719053b376.description: This tutorial explains how to use the XCFolder tool I developed to convert virtual directories in XCode projects into physical directories, improving directory structure confusion and enhancing integration with modern tools like XCodeGen and Tuist.

step.name: Step 1: Download the XCFolder Project Source Code
step.text: Enter git clone https://github.com/ZhgChgLi/XCFolder.git in the terminal to download the XCFolder project source code to your local directory.

step.name: Step 2: Enter the XCFolder Project Directory
step.text: Use the command cd ./XCFolder to switch to the XCFolder project folder you just downloaded, preparing to run the tool.

step.name: Step 3: Run the XCFolder Conversion Command
step.text: Enter swift run XCFolder YOUR_XCODEPROJ_FILE.xcodeproj ./Configuration.yaml, replacing YOUR_XCODEPROJ_FILE.xcodeproj with the path of the XCode project file you want to convert, and specify the Configuration.yaml configuration file.

step.name: Step 4: Run in Non-Interactive Mode (for CI/CD)
step.text: If running in a CI/CD environment, use the command swift run XCFolder YOUR_XCODEPROJ_FILE.xcodeproj ./Configuration.yaml --is-non-interactive-mode to enable non-interactive mode for automatic conversion.

step.name: Step 5: Verify if the Conversion Was Successful
step.text: After execution, check if any files are missing in the project directory, confirm whether the ignorePaths and ignoreFileTypes settings in Configuration.yaml are correct, and try Clean & Build the project to ensure the directory structure has been successfully converted into physical directories.

71400d408dc8.name: How to Use Google Apps Script to Connect to Firebase App Distribution API
71400d408dc8.description: This article demonstrates how to configure and connect to the Firebase App Distribution API in Google Apps Script, including GCP project setup, enabling advanced services, modifying appsscript.json, and writing integration code.

step.name: Step 1: Create and Configure GCP Project
step.text: Create a new project in Google Cloud Platform or use an existing Firebase project, note the project number, and ensure the Firebase App Distribution API is enabled for the project. Confirm that the current Google account has permission to use the project and API.

step.name: Step 2: Associate GCP Project in Google Apps Script
step.text: Open the Google Apps Script editor, and in the project settings, enter the previously obtained GCP project number into the Google Cloud Platform project field to complete the association.

step.name: Step 3: Enable Advanced Services and Modify appsscript.json
step.text: Enable advanced services in the Google Apps Script editor, and enable displaying the appsscript.json configuration file in the file list. Open appsscript.json and ensure that oauthScopes includes "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/script.external_request" and "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"; if not, manually add them and save.

step.name: Step 4: Write Code to Connect to Firebase App Distribution API
step.text: Write code in the Google Apps Script editor, replacing the Firebase Project ID and App ID in the corresponding places in the code. Use UrlFetchApp along with ScriptApp.getOAuthToken() to obtain authorization, call the Firebase App Distribution API, and handle the returned data.

step.name: Step 5: Run the Script and Complete Permission Authorization
step.text: When running the script for the first time, the system will request authorization. Click "Review Permissions", select the executing account, expand advanced options and click "Go to XXX", then finally click "Allow" to authorize. After authorization is complete, run the script again.

step.name: Step 6: Verify Successful Connection
step.text: After running the script, observe the execution logs or Logger output to confirm whether the Firebase App Distribution release version and download link were successfully retrieved. If displayed correctly, the connection is successful.

7508328d8b8d.name: How to Test Release Environment Optimization Settings After Upgrading XCode
7508328d8b8d.description: When encountering ghost crashes or logic issues that only appear in the Build Configuration Release, test whether the problem can be reproduced by setting the Debug environment's Optimization Level to the Release setting.

step.name: Step 1: Open the XCode Project and Select the App Target
step.text: Open XCode and click on your App Target in the project navigator to prepare for adjusting build settings.

step.name: Step 2: Go to Build Settings and Search for Optimization Level
step.text: In the App Target settings page, switch to the Build Settings tab and use the search box to enter 'Optimization Level' to quickly locate the setting.

step.name: Step 3: Set the Debug Environment's Optimization Level to the Release Value
step.text: Find the Optimization Level setting block and change the Debug environment value to match the Release environment, for example, set it to Fastest, Smallest [-Os], to simulate Release build optimizations.

step.name: Step 4: Build & Run the Project to Test for Anomalies
step.text: After completing the settings, perform Build & Run in XCode and observe whether the app exhibits the crashes or logic issues previously seen only in Release mode while running in Debug mode.

step.name: Step 5: Verify Whether the Release Environment Issue Is Successfully Reproduced
step.text: If the issue from Release mode is successfully reproduced in Debug mode, it indicates that optimization settings affect program behavior, allowing further debugging or code adjustments to resolve the problem.

404bd5c70040.name: How to Use GitHub Actions with Self-hosted Runner to Build Automated Workflows
404bd5c70040.description: This tutorial guides you from architectural understanding to practical operation, including creating a workflow for automatic labeling of Pull Request file change sizes, automatic assignee assignment, daily PR statistics with Slack notifications, and how to set up and start a Self-hosted Runner.

step.name: Step 1: Create a Workflow to Automatically Label Pull Request File Changes Size
step.text: On the GitHub Repo's Actions page, click New workflow, select set up a workflow yourself, and create a file named Automation-PullRequest.yml. Set the trigger events to pull_request opened, synchronize, and reopened, and write a Job using the ubuntu-latest Runner. In the Steps, use a shell script with the gh CLI to get the number of changed files in the PR, then determine and apply the corresponding Label based on the count. After completion, commit to the main branch; opening a new PR will automatically trigger the workflow.

step.name: Step 2: Create a Workflow to Automatically Assign the Author and Comment When Pull Request Has No Assignee
step.text: In the same Automation-PullRequest.yml Workflow, add a new Job with the condition of pull_request event and action opened. The Job uses the ubuntu-latest Runner. In the Steps, use actions/github-script@v7 to write a JavaScript script that checks if the PR has an assignee; if not, it automatically assigns the PR author and posts a notification comment. After committing and pushing, this task will automatically run when a new PR is created without an assignee.

step.name: Step 3: Create a Daily Scheduled Workflow to Summarize PRs, Send Slack Notifications, and Automatically Close PRs Older Than 90 Days
step.text: Create a Workflow named Automation-PullRequest-Daily.yml, set the trigger events to daily at 01:00 UTC (9 AM Taiwan time) and manual trigger. The caculate-pr-status Job uses actions/github-script@v7 to retrieve all open PRs and calculate their open days, then outputs the results. Next, the send-pr-summary-message-to-slack Job depends on the previous Job, uses github-script to generate the Slack message format, and sends the message via slackapi/slack-github-action@v2.1.0. The auto-close-old-prs Job also depends on caculate-pr-status, uses github-script to automatically close PRs older than 90 days and posts a comment.

step.name: Step 4: Configure and Start the Self-hosted Runner
step.text: In the GitHub Repo settings page, go to Actions → Runners → New self-hosted runner, select macOS ARM64 architecture, then follow instructions to download and extract the Runner archive. Run the ./config.sh command, enter the Repo URL and Token, set the Runner name and custom Label (e.g., self-hosted-zhgchgli). After configuration, run ./run.sh to start the Runner. When the Terminal shows Connected to GitHub and Listening for Jobs, the Runner is started and waiting for tasks.

step.name: Step 5: Change Workflow runs-on to Use Self-hosted Runner Label
step.text: In the Automation-PullRequest.yml Workflow, change all Jobs' runs-on from ubuntu-latest to the custom Self-hosted Runner Label, for example runs-on: [self-hosted-zhgchgli]. After committing and pushing, when a PR triggers the Workflow, the tasks will be dispatched to the self-hosted Runner for execution.

step.name: Step 6: Verify Whether the Workflow Runs Successfully
step.text: Open a new Pull Request or manually trigger the daily summary Workflow, then go to the GitHub Actions page to check the Workflow execution status. Confirm that the tasks run on the self-hosted Runner without errors. Check if the PR is automatically labeled with the Size Label, the author is automatically assigned and commented, and whether Slack receives the daily PR summary message. Successful display indicates the setup is complete.

4b001d2e8440.name: How to Implement iOS App CI/CD Workflow Using GitHub Actions
4b001d2e8440.description: This tutorial provides a detailed explanation on how to use GitHub Actions to build a continuous integration (CI) and continuous deployment (CD) workflow for iOS apps, including environment setup, dependency installation, test execution, packaging and deployment to Firebase App Distribution, as well as automated nightly workflows.

step.name: Step 1: Set Up Project Dependencies and Environment
step.text: Use a Makefile to uniformly manage project dependencies. First, execute git clone to download the project. After switching to the project directory, run make setup to install necessary tool dependencies (such as brew, mint, bundle, etc.), then run make install to generate project files and install CocoaPods dependencies.

step.name: Step 2: Create GitHub Actions CI Workflow
step.text: Create a new workflow on the GitHub Repo's Actions page and write CI-Testing.yml. Set the trigger conditions to Pull Request opened, synchronized, or reopened. In the workflow, perform checkout, read .xcode-version and .ruby-version, set up the Ruby environment, cache dependencies, run make setup and make install, and finally use Fastlane to execute the specified test lane.

step.name: Step 3: Create GitHub Actions CD Workflow
step.text: Create the CD-Deploy.yml workflow with manual form triggers allowing input of version number, Build Number, and Release Note. The process includes checking out code, importing Apple certificates and Provisioning Profiles, reading Xcode and Ruby versions, installing dependency caches, running make setup and make install, and finally using Fastlane beta lane to package and deploy to Firebase App Distribution.

step.name: Step 4: Create Nightly Automated CI/CD Workflow
step.text: Create CI-Nightly-Build-And-Deploy.yml, setting a scheduled trigger at 3 AM daily. First, run the CI-Testing.yml test workflow, then obtain a date string as the version number. After successful testing, call CD-Deploy.yml to package and deploy, and send Slack notifications about the test and deployment status based on the results.

step.name: Step 5: Verify CI/CD Workflow Success
step.text: Submit a Pull Request to trigger the CI workflow, confirm test reports and build status, with failure comments left on the PR indicating errors. Manually trigger the CD workflow, check if packaging and deployment succeed, and verify if a new version appears on Firebase App Distribution. The nightly workflow runs automatically and notifies results via Slack, confirming the overall workflow operates correctly.

4273e57e7148.name: How to Use Google Apps Script Web App to Integrate with GitHub Actions to Build a Free and Easy Packaging Tool Platform
4273e57e7148.description: This tutorial demonstrates how to create a Web App based on Google Apps Script, integrate it with GitHub Actions, and build a cross-team shared packaging tool platform. It includes the complete process of project creation, code copying, deployment settings, integration with GitHub and Slack, and obtaining Firebase App Distribution download links.

step.name: Step 1: Create a Google Apps Script Project and Enable appsscript.json
step.text: Go to https://script.google.com/home to create a new Google Apps Script project and name it. In the project settings, check "Show 'appsscript.json' manifest file in editor" to facilitate subsequent Metadata configuration.

step.name: Step 2: Copy and Create Example Project Files
step.text: Refer to the open-source example project, create all files according to the example, and copy the content directly, including appsscript.json, Entrypoint.gs, Controller_iOS.gs, View_iOS.html, etc., ensuring the project structure matches the example.

step.name: Step 3: Deploy the Web App and Set Access Permissions
step.text: Click "Deploy" → "New deployment" at the top right of the project, select the type as "Web app", set the execution identity to yourself, and set access permissions to "Anyone in your organization". After deployment, obtain the Web App URL.

step.name: Step 4: Integrate GitHub API and Insert Token
step.text: Create a Fine-grained personal access token in your GitHub account settings with permissions including Repo Actions (read/write) and Administration (read only). Insert this token into the githubToken variable in Credentials.gs of the GAS project, and replace GitHubStub with GitHub.

step.name: Step 5: Integrate Slack Notification Feature
step.text: Create a Slack Bot App and obtain the Bot Token. Insert the token into the slackBotToken variable in Credentials.gs of the GAS project, change SlackStub to Slack in Settings.gs, and add CD-Deploy-Form.yml in the GitHub Actions Workflow to configure Slack notification steps.

step.name: Step 6: Deploy and Test Web App Functionality
step.text: Refresh the test Web App URL, confirm the packaging form displays correctly, fill in the information, and click "Submit Request". If a success message appears and the packaging record shows task execution, the integration is successful.

step.name: Step 7: Verify Successful Integration and Obtain Download Link
step.text: After completing Firebase App Distribution integration settings, click "Get Download Link" in the packaging record. If you can see and click the download link directing to the Firebase download page, the integration is successful and the entire packaging tool platform is fully operational.

ba132457e6a5.name: How to Install and Enable Google Offerwall Ads on Your Website
ba132457e6a5.description: This tutorial explains how to embed Google Adsense dynamic ad code into your website, create and test Google Offerwall reward wall ads, enabling content creators to monetize through rewarded ads.

step.name: Step 1: Obtain and Embed Google Adsense Dynamic Ad Code
step.text: Go to the Google Adsense website, click on "Ads" and then select "Sites" to obtain the dynamic ad code. Paste this code into the <head></head> section of your website's HTML to ensure the ad script loads correctly.

step.name: Step 2: Create Google Offerwall Ad Message
step.text: Log in to Google Adsense, navigate to the "Privacy & Messaging" page, click "Manage" and then select "Create Message." On this page, set the Offerwall ad name, specify the website, include or exclude webpage paths, and configure the default language and trigger conditions.

step.name: Step 3: Configure Reward Content and Copy Style
step.text: On the create message page, set the reward content after task completion, such as unlimited viewing for 24 hours after completing the task. Then upload the brand logo and adjust the copy text style and color to ensure the ad appearance matches your website's style.

step.name: Step 4: Publish and Confirm Ad Status
step.text: After completing all settings, click the "Publish Changes" button to officially launch the Offerwall ad message. Confirm the ad status is published to ensure the ad functions properly.

step.name: Step 5: Test Offerwall Ads Using Incognito Mode
step.text: Use Chrome's incognito window, disable all ad blockers and anti-tracking extensions, then navigate to the configured webpage path to test ad triggering and task completion flow. Confirm that the reward wall displays correctly and that content unlocks after task completion, indicating successful installation.

e4d139fe0685.name: How to Deposit to Firstrade Using LINE Bank and Apply for Fee Reimbursement
e4d139fe0685.description: This tutorial provides detailed instructions on how to transfer funds from LINE Bank to a Firstrade account, including obtaining transfer information, operating the LINE Bank App, account upgrading and binding designated accounts, as well as the complete process for applying for Firstrade transfer fee reimbursement.

step.name: Step 1: Log in to Firstrade and Obtain Transfer Information
step.text: Log in to Firstrade on a computer, click "Customer Service" then select "Account Funding", then click "Wire Transfer" → "Wire Transfer Information". The system will open a PDF file; please save this file for reference. It contains necessary transfer information such as the beneficiary bank's SWIFT code, beneficiary name, address, account number, and reference message.

step.name: Step 2: Use LINE Bank App to Make an Overseas Transfer
step.text: Open the LINE Bank App, tap "More", scroll down to find the "Foreign Exchange" section and select "Transfer Overseas". Choose the debit currency (TWD or USD) and enter the transfer amount, then tap "Proceed to Transfer". Fill in the beneficiary's English name, SWIFT code, beneficiary account number, address, and most importantly, the reference message field according to the information provided by Firstrade. Select the transfer purpose as "262 Investment in Foreign Equity Securities", confirm all details are correct, and submit the transfer application.

step.name: Step 3: Check LINE Bank Transfer Status and Download Transfer Receipt
step.text: In the LINE Bank App, find the foreign currency transfer notification in the top right notifications area and tap to view the transfer status. Transfers on business days are usually completed the same day; transfers on holidays will be processed on the next business day. If you need a transfer receipt, scroll down on the transfer status page and select "Request Receipt/Transaction Certificate". The system will send the receipt to your registered email, which can be opened using your ID number.

step.name: Step 4: Upgrade LINE Bank Account and Bind Designated Account
step.text: Open the LINE Bank App, tap "More" → "Others" → "Account Upgrade", select the upgrade type and complete the account upgrade via video verification. Then add a designated account by tapping "More" → "Foreign Exchange" → "My Transfer Records" → "Designated Transfer Account" → "Add Designated Account". For the first binding, complete natural person certificate verification and video verification. Enter the Firstrade transfer information and complete two-factor SMS verification. Wait about 2 days for the designated account to become effective.

step.name: Step 5: Apply for Firstrade Transfer Fee Reimbursement
step.text: Log in to Firstrade, click "Customer Service" → "Special Offers" → "Transfer Fee Subsidy", select the eligible transfer record (single transfer amount must exceed 10,000 USD), fill in the remitting financial institution name as LINE Bank Taiwan Limited, enter the transfer fee charged by the financial institution as 25 USD, and submit the application. The reimbursement will be credited in about 2-3 business days and can be checked under "My Account" → "Account Records".

step.name: Step 6: Verify Whether the Transfer Was Successful
step.text: Log in to Firstrade, go to "My Account" to check if the account records show the deposited funds, confirming the funds have been successfully transferred. If the LINE Bank App shows "Beneficiary Bank Has Received Funds" but Firstrade does not yet show the deposit, please wait patiently for processing on U.S. business days; usually, you will receive a deposit notification email and see the funds credited the next day.

bea62c251f1b.name: How to Use Rakuten Forwarding to Purchase Japanese Products and Ship to Taiwan
bea62c251f1b.description: A detailed tutorial on how to register a Rakuten account, complete Rakuten Forwarding real-name authentication, order products from Rakuten Ichiba Japan and Amazon JP, and ship them back to Taiwan via Rakuten Forwarding consolidation service.

step.name: Step 1: Register a Japanese Rakuten Account
step.text: Go to the Rakuten registration page, enter your email address, password, and English name, then submit to complete the account registration. If you encounter server errors, switch the website language to Japanese to resolve the issue.

step.name: Step 2: Complete Rakuten Forwarding Real-Name Authentication
step.text: Log in to the Rakuten Forwarding website, switch the language to Traditional Chinese, and click on My Page to activate the forwarding service. Receive the verification code via email to complete email verification, then fill in your Chinese name and name in Katakana. Next, agree to the user terms, enter your real passport English name and date of birth, and complete the activation of the forwarding address.

step.name: Step 3: Perform Online Facial Recognition Authentication
step.text: Prepare your original ID card and mobile phone, and complete the 24-hour self-service facial recognition authentication through the Rakuten Forwarding website. If authentication fails, the system will automatically switch to manual review, and you will receive a notification of completion the next day.

step.name: Step 4: Place Orders on Rakuten Ichiba or Amazon JP
step.text: Use the dedicated recipient address and phone number provided by Rakuten Forwarding to select products on Rakuten Ichiba Japan or Amazon JP. Fill in the forwarding address at checkout, confirm product specifications, add items to the cart, and complete payment.

step.name: Step 5: Register Shipment Information for Non-Rakuten Platform Purchases
step.text: If you purchase products outside Rakuten Ichiba, click on the shipment list on the Rakuten Forwarding website, select Register/Confirm, enter the store name, product information, and amount to complete the pre-registration of shipment arrival.

step.name: Step 6: Submit Packaging Request and Choose Packaging Services
step.text: After the goods arrive at the Rakuten Forwarding warehouse, click on the shipment list to submit a packaging request. Confirm the contents for combined packaging, then choose whether to remove original packaging, waterproof the package, and use international shipping reinforced boxes and other packaging services.

step.name: Step 7: Pay Shipping Fees and Complete Shipping Request
step.text: Once packaging is complete, go to the pending payment page in the shipment list and click to proceed with payment. Confirm the shipment contents and recipient information are correct, select the shipping method (such as EMS express air), enter credit card or Paypal information to complete payment, and Rakuten Forwarding will arrange shipment back to Taiwan.

step.name: Step 8: Verify Successful Receipt of Products
step.text: After payment, wait for the Rakuten Forwarding shipping notification and obtain the tracking number. Upon receiving the package, confirm the products are intact and meet the order specifications, indicating successful completion of the process of purchasing from Rakuten Ichiba Japan or Amazon JP and shipping back to Taiwan.

62f68ebeecd3.name: How to Safely Use iOS DispatchSourceTimer and Encapsulate a Finite State Machine
62f68ebeecd3.description: This tutorial explains how to use a finite state machine to encapsulate DispatchSourceTimer, preventing crashes caused by incorrect operations, and demonstrates how to safely use it in a multithreaded environment.

step.name: Step 1: Define the TimerState States of the Finite State Machine
step.text: Define the TimerState enum in the DispatchSourceTimerMachine class, including four states: idle, running, suspended, and cancelled, representing different lifecycle stages of the timer.

step.name: Step 2: Implement the activate Method to Start the Timer
step.text: Implement the activate(repeatTimeInterval:handler:) method in DispatchSourceTimerMachine, which creates a DispatchSourceTimer only when in idle or cancelled state, sets the schedule and event handler, calls activate() to start the timer, and switches the state to running.

step.name: Step 3: Implement suspend, resume, and cancel Methods to Control Timer State
step.text: Implement suspend() to pause the timer (available only in running state), resume() to resume the timer (available only in suspended state), and cancel() to terminate the timer (available only in suspended or running state; if suspended, call resume() before cancelling), updating the state accordingly in sequence.

step.name: Step 4: Use a Serial DispatchQueue to Ensure Safe State Transitions
step.text: Create a dedicated Serial DispatchQueue in DispatchSourceTimerMachine, and perform all state changes and timer operations synchronously or asynchronously through this queue to avoid race conditions and crashes caused by concurrent multithreaded operations.

step.name: Step 5: Verify Successful Usage of DispatchSourceTimerMachine
step.text: In the class using DispatchSourceTimerMachine, call activate() to start the scheduled task, use suspend() to pause, resume() to continue, and cancel() to stop, then observe whether timer events are triggered as expected without crashes, indicating successful encapsulation and safe usage.

7c0974856393.name: How to Use App Store Connect API Webhook to Integrate Automated Workflows
7c0974856393.description: This tutorial explains how to create and configure a Webhook in App Store Connect, verify Webhook requests, and integrate CI/CD automated workflows to improve development efficiency.

step.name: Step 1: Create an App Store Connect API Webhook
step.text: Go to the "Users and Access" section in the App Store Connect admin panel, then to the "Integrations" page. Click "Webhooks" under "Other Integrations", then click the "Create Webhook" button. Enter the Webhook name, the hosting URL (Webhook receiving service URL), and the secret string. Select the App to receive notifications for, check the required trigger events, and finally click "Add" to create the Webhook.

step.name: Step 2: Test the App Store Connect API Webhook Notification
step.text: Go to the Webhook page and click the "Test" button at the top right to receive a test notification. The system will send an HTTP POST request containing event data to your Webhook URL. Confirm that your service can correctly receive and process this test notification.

step.name: Step 3: Verify the Authenticity of the Webhook Request
step.text: Use the secret string you set to perform an HMAC-SHA256 operation on the received Request Body, then convert the result to a HEX string. Compare this string with the value in the x-apple-signature header of the HTTP request to ensure they match and verify the request's authenticity. You can refer to example code in Node.js or Cloudflare Worker to implement this verification.

step.name: Step 4: Integrate CI/CD Automated Workflows
step.text: After the Webhook Endpoint receives a notification, forward the event data to CI/CD services (such as GitHub Actions, Bitbucket Pipeline, etc.) to trigger corresponding automation scripts. You can use Fastlane Spaceship to call the App Store Connect API to obtain complete event information and execute subsequent automated steps, such as sending notifications or triggering other workflows.

step.name: Step 5: Verify Whether the Webhook Automation Workflow Succeeded
step.text: Confirm that the CI/CD service successfully received the Webhook event and performed the corresponding actions. You can check the "Recent Submissions" section at the bottom of the Webhook page to view the latest event records and verify in the automation system whether the related workflows were successfully triggered.

88f0fb935120.name: How to Use Medium Private API to Scrape Data and Bypass Cloudflare Protection
88f0fb935120.description: This tutorial explains how to sniff Medium Private API requests using browser developer tools, call the API with Postman or code to obtain follower counts, article lists, and article content, and demonstrates how to use Cloudflare Worker to avoid being blocked by Cloudflare.

step.name: Step 1: Sniff Medium Graphql API Requests Using Browser Developer Tools
step.text: Open Chrome browser, right-click on a blank area of the page and select "Inspect" to open developer tools, switch to the Network tab, check Preserve log, and enter graphql in the search bar. Then go to the Medium account followers page https://medium.com/@YOUR_USER_NAME/followers, observe the requests with graphql in the left request list, and search for the keyword followerCount in the Response to find the request containing the target username and follower count.

step.name: Step 2: Copy the Target Graphql Request Payload and Test with Postman
step.text: Click the found graphql request, switch to Payload -> View Source, and copy the entire JSON content. Open Postman, create a POST request with the URL set to https://medium.com/_/graphql, select raw for the Body and paste the copied JSON Payload, set Content-Type to application/json, and send the request to obtain follower count data.

step.name: Step 3: Call Medium Private API to Get Follower Count Using Ruby Code
step.text: In Ruby code, use Net::HTTP to establish an HTTPS connection, set User-Agent and Content-Type headers, and put the JSON-formatted Payload into the POST request body. Call the https://medium.com/_/graphql API, parse the response JSON, and get the follower count from response[0]["data"]["userResult"]["socialStats"]["followerCount"].

step.name: Step 4: Call Medium Private API to Get Account Article List
step.text: Similarly, create a POST request to https://medium.com/_/graphql, paste the UserProfileQuery Graphql Query JSON in the Body, and include the account User Id. After sending the request, obtain the article list information from response[0]["data"]["userResult"]["homepagePostsConnection"]["posts"].

step.name: Step 5: Call Medium Private API to Get Specific Article Content
step.text: Create a POST request to https://medium.com/_/graphql, paste the PostViewerEdgeContentQuery Graphql Query JSON in the Body, and include the article ID. After sending the request, get the article paragraphs JSON from response[0]["data"]["post"]["viewerEdge"]["fullContent"]["bodyModel"]["paragraphs"] and assemble the complete article content.

step.name: Step 6: Deploy Cloudflare Worker to Proxy Medium API Requests to Bypass Protection
step.text: Write a fetch event handler in Cloudflare Worker to intercept requests with the path /graphql, parse the request JSON, forward the POST request to https://medium.com/_/graphql, and include the original request's Cookie and User-Agent headers. Return the Medium API response JSON to the caller to avoid being blocked by Cloudflare Bot protection.

step.name: Step 7: Verify Successful Retrieval of Medium Follower Count and Article Content
step.text: Use Postman or custom code to call the deployed Cloudflare Worker URL, send the Graphql request, and confirm the response contains the correct follower count, article list, and article content data. Successfully obtaining the data indicates that Cloudflare protection has been bypassed and Medium Private API data can be scraped.

823ac523ccc8.name: How to Use Fastlane Match to Manage iOS Certificates Uniformly and Integrate CI/CD Workflow
823ac523ccc8.description: Introduction on how to create a Fastlane Match Git repository, configure and use Fastlane Match to manage iOS development and distribution certificates, and integrate it into the GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow.

step.name: Step 1: Create a Git Private Match Repository
step.text: Create an empty private repository on GitHub to securely store all certificates and provisioning profiles. Ensure the repository access permissions are properly set and prepare to access it via SSH.

step.name: Step 2: Configure Local Git SSH Access Permissions
step.text: Set up Git SSH access permissions on your local machine and verify you can successfully clone the newly created Match repository using the command git clone git@github.com:xxx/certificates.git.

step.name: Step 3: Run fastlane match Initialization in Project Directory
step.text: Run bundle exec fastlane match init in the project directory, select git as the storage mode, and enter the SSH URL of the newly created Git Private Match repository to generate the Matchfile.

step.name: Step 4: Generate App Store Connect API .p8 Key
step.text: Generate the App Store Connect API .p8 key from the Apple Developer portal and place this key in the project's fastlane directory for Fastlane to use in managing certificates.

step.name: Step 5: Use fastlane match to Create and Upload Development Certificates and Profiles
step.text: Run the command bundle exec fastlane match_development type:development isRead:false. Fastlane will create Development Certificates and Profiles, encrypt them, and push them to the Match Git repository.

step.name: Step 6: Other Developers or CI/CD Services Pull Certificates
step.text: Other team members or CI/CD services run bundle exec fastlane match_development type:development to pull and install certificates and provisioning profiles from the Match Git repository into the local keychain.

step.name: Step 7: Verify Successful Pull and Installation of Certificates
step.text: Run the command security find-identity -v -p codesigning locally to confirm that the installed certificate list includes the corresponding Development or Distribution certificates, indicating successful certificate management.

6bf79c5b4dab.name: How to Create a Custom Jekyll Blog Theme Using Claude Design and Claude Code
6bf79c5b4dab.description: This tutorial explains how to start by creating a clean Jekyll Blog, use Claude Design to design the blog style, implement the design with Claude Code, and finally complete and verify the blog theme.

step.name: Step 1: Create a Clean Jekyll Blog
step.text: Enter the command jekyll new blog in the terminal to create a new Jekyll Blog project, which will include the default blog structure and files.

step.name: Step 2: Collect and Organize Existing Theme Page Examples
step.text: On the current blog pages, right-click each page such as Home, Post, Page, PostList, Archives, Tags, and additional files to save them individually. Then create a Theme directory and place all pages and files into that directory.

step.name: Step 3: Upload the Theme Directory to Claude Design and Start Designing
step.text: Go to https://claude.ai/design to create a new project, select the "Attach codebase" feature, upload the organized Theme directory, then enter the design requirements prompt and ask Claude Design to redesign the blog pages for you.

step.name: Step 4: Adjust Claude Design Results
step.text: Use the Tweak panel at the bottom right of Claude Design to adjust parameters such as fonts, layout width, primary color, and paper color. You can also click the top right of the page to zoom in and view the full webpage, confirm interactive functions and display status, and ask the AI to make functional or layout adjustments if necessary.

step.name: Step 5: Download the Design Files and Place Them into the Jekyll Blog Directory
step.text: After completing the design, click "Share" → "Download project as .zip" in the top right corner to download the design files. Extract the folder and place it into your local Jekyll Blog project directory to prepare for further implementation.

step.name: Step 6: Implement the Design Using Claude Code
step.text: Run the /init command in the Jekyll Blog directory to let Claude Code understand the project structure. Then ask Claude Code to apply the ./MyBlogTheme theme design to the blog and wait for the AI to complete the build.

step.name: Step 7: Start the Local Server and Verify the Design Success
step.text: Run the command bundle exec jekyll serve to start the local server. Open a browser and go to http://127.0.0.1:4000/ to confirm that the blog pages have applied the new design and that all functions work properly, indicating the theme creation was successful.

35cc65327d28.name: How to Let AI Agent Develop Google Apps Script Integration and Dashboard for You
35cc65327d28.description: By installing the clasp tool, designing the Dashboard, setting up the API Key and Google Sheet, and using Claude Design and Claude Code, let the AI Agent automatically develop and deploy the Google Apps Script Web App.

step.name: Step 1: Install the clasp CLI tool
step.text: First, ensure Node.js version 20.0.0 or above is installed. Then, enter the command npm i @google/clasp -g in the terminal to install the Google Apps Script command-line tool clasp.

step.name: Step 2: Log in to Google account using clasp
step.text: Run clasp login in the terminal. A browser window will open the Google login page. Choose the Google account to use and authorize it. After completion, an authorization token will be generated.

step.name: Step 3: Save the authorization token to macOS Keychain
step.text: Run the command security add-generic-password -U -s "com.google.clasp" -a "$USER" -w "$(cat ~/.clasprc.json)" in the terminal to securely store the authorization information in the system Keychain, making it convenient for the AI Agent to access.

step.name: Step 4: Create project directory and initialize Google Apps Script project
step.text: Create a project folder locally and switch to that directory. Run the clasp create command, which will create a new Google Apps Script project named after the folder and generate the appsscript.json configuration file.

step.name: Step 5: Design the Dashboard interface using Claude Design or Design Agent Skill
step.text: Use Claude Design or third-party design skills to design the Dashboard according to your preferred style. Export the design files into the ./Design folder within the project directory and preview the design results locally by opening the .html files.

step.name: Step 6: Register for the Ministry of Transportation and Communications Central Weather Bureau API Key and create a countdown Google Sheet
step.text: Go to https://opendata.cwa.gov.tw/userLogin to register and obtain an API Key. Create a Google Sheet containing event titles, dates, and recurrence information. Note the Sheet ID and GID to use as data sources for the Dashboard.

step.name: Step 7: Let AI Agent develop and deploy the Web App using Claude Code
step.text: Initialize Claude Code memory and run the /plan command to plan the development tasks. Provide the API Key, Sheet ID, and GID to let the AI Agent automatically develop the Google Apps Script code. Use clasp push and deployment commands to complete the Web App deployment.

step.name: Step 8: Manually authorize and complete deployment setup
step.text: The first run requires manually selecting and running any function in the Script Editor to complete the OAuth authorization process. After this, the AI Agent can run automatically, completing the Web App deployment and testing.

step.name: Step 9: Verify success
step.text: Open the deployed Web App URL, enter the correct token, and confirm that the Dashboard correctly displays the Ministry of Transportation weather data, calendar events, and countdown data. This indicates the AI Agent has successfully completed the Google Apps Script integration and development.

cdc.name: How to Apply for and Use the Citizen Digital Certificate
cdc.description: This article provides detailed instructions on the application process for the Citizen Digital Certificate, activation and security settings after card collection, and how to use the certificate for identity verification and digital signatures on computers and mobile phones.

step.name: Step 1: Fill Out the Citizen Digital Certificate Application Online
step.text: Open the official MOICA website's Citizen Digital Certificate application form (https://moica.nat.gov.tw/rac_form.html) and fill in fields such as name, ID number, email, mobile phone number, PIN code, and user code. After completing the form, please note that the application data is only retained for 7 days, so be sure to schedule a visit to the household registration office for in-person processing.

step.name: Step 2: Visit the Household Registration Office for In-Person Application
step.text: Bring your original National ID card and cash for the processing fee to any household registration office for in-person application. On-site, your identity will be verified, payment collected, and the PIN code for the Citizen Digital Certificate will be set or confirmed. Finally, you will receive the physical Citizen Digital Certificate card.

step.name: Step 3: Activate and Configure Security Settings After Receiving the Card
step.text: After receiving the Citizen Digital Certificate card, do not rush to use it. First, set up security management tasks, including changing the default PIN code, properly safeguarding the user code, and referring to the official security usage checklist to ensure the certificate's security.

step.name: Step 4: Install the Card Reader and Related Software on Your Computer
step.text: Purchase a USB card reader that supports smart cards and plug it into your computer. According to the instructions of the website you intend to use, download and install the cross-platform web component or driver from the official MOICA website (https://moica.nat.gov.tw/rac_plugin.html). After installation, restart your browser or computer to ensure the component functions properly.

step.name: Step 5: Use the Citizen Digital Certificate to Complete Identity Verification on Your Computer
step.text: Insert the Citizen Digital Certificate into the card reader, open the website requiring verification, and select login with the Citizen Digital Certificate. Follow the website prompts to enter your PIN code to complete verification or signing, then remove the card after completion.

step.name: Step 6: Use Mobile Phone NFC to Authenticate the Citizen Digital Certificate
step.text: Ensure your mobile phone supports NFC and that the service provider and the Citizen Digital Certificate card version support NFC. Hold the Citizen Digital Certificate card close to the designated sensing area on the back of your phone, slowly move to find the optimal sensing point, keep the card flat against the phone, and hold it there until verification is complete.

step.name: Step 7: Verify Successful Use of the Citizen Digital Certificate
step.text: After completing login or signing with the Citizen Digital Certificate on your computer or mobile phone, confirm that the website displays a successful login or signing completion message. If using a card reader, ensure the reader properly reads the card and the PIN code is entered correctly; if using mobile NFC, confirm the sensing is smooth and no error messages appear.

tax.name: How to Complete 2026 Online Tax Filing Using Citizen Digital Certificate or Mobile Citizen Digital Certificate
tax.description: This article explains how to use the Citizen Digital Certificate or Mobile Citizen Digital Certificate to complete the 2026 comprehensive income tax online filing, including application process, component installation, tax filing operation steps, and verification of success.

step.name: Step 1: Apply for Citizen Digital Certificate
step.text: First, fill out the Citizen Digital Certificate application form online (https://moica.nat.gov.tw/rac_form.html). Within 7 days after filling out the form, bring your original ID card to any household registration office in person to complete the procedure, pay a 250 NTD processing fee, set a password, and receive the certificate card.

step.name: Step 2: Install Tax Filing System Components
step.text: According to your operating system and browser requirements, go to the comprehensive income tax electronic filing and payment system homepage (https://efile.tax.nat.gov.tw/) to download and install the tax filing components, and install the MOICA cross-platform web component (https://moica.nat.gov.tw/rac_plugin.html). After installation, restart your browser.

step.name: Step 3: Use Citizen Digital Certificate to File Taxes
step.text: Insert the Citizen Digital Certificate into the USB card reader, open https://efile.tax.nat.gov.tw/, select the comprehensive income tax electronic filing and payment system, choose "Citizen Digital Certificate" for identity verification, and enter the PIN code. The system will automatically download income and deduction data.

step.name: Step 4: Edit and Calculate Tax Filing Data
step.text: After confirming and editing the income and deduction data, perform the calculation function, select the appropriate payment method (credit card, mobile payment, demand deposit deduction, chip financial card, or convenience store payment), and complete the preparation of the filing data.

step.name: Step 5: Upload Filing and Obtain Receipt
step.text: Upload the filing data to the system. After completing the filing, download and save the receipt PDF as proof of tax filing completion. Finally, remove the Citizen Digital Certificate card.

step.name: Step 6: Verify Tax Filing Success
step.text: Log in to the Ministry of Finance Tax Portal (https://www.etax.nat.gov.tw/etwmain/), enter your ID number and verification code, check the status of comprehensive income tax filing and refund payment, and confirm that the filing data has been successfully submitted and recorded by the system.
